  • [ ] Install to home screen
  • [ ] Offline shell

docs.title: Technical Notes
docs.text:
• Single file: src/icom_lan/web/static/index.html (2695 lines)
  • All mobile changes gated behind @media (max-width: 767px) or .mobile-only class
  • Desktop layout (≥768px) completely unchanged
  • Tailwind CSS + vanilla JS (no framework)

docs.title: ✅ #103: SDRplay RSPdx wideband panadapter
docs.text: Labels: enhancement

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Use SDRplay RSPdx as a wideband panadapter alongside the main transceiver. Second waterfall showing entire band (e.g. full 40m), click-to-tune the transceiver.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text: IC-7610 scope shows ±25/50/100 kHz. RSPdx can show up to 10 MHz bandwidth — the entire band at once. Clicking a signal on the wide waterfall tunes the transceiver.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] SoapySDR Python bindings for RSPdx
  • [ ] FFT pipeline: IQ samples → spectrum data → WebSocket
  • [ ] Second waterfall canvas in web UI (above or below radio scope)
  • [ ] Click-to-tune: click on wide waterfall → set transceiver frequency
  • [ ] Band tracking: wide waterfall follows transceiver band changes
  • [ ] Configurable: center freq, bandwidth, FFT size, averaging
  • [ ] Optional: signal markers (overlay DX spots, band plan)

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• SDRplay RSPdx available
  • SoapySDR + SoapySDRPlay driver
  • Antenna: can share via splitter or use separate RX antenna

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• numpy (FFT)
  • soapysdr (SDR access)
  • Existing waterfall renderer (Canvas API) can be reused

docs.title: ⬜ #104: WebRTC audio for lower latency
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P3

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Replace custom WebSocket audio transport with WebRTC for significantly lower latency and better browser audio handling.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text:
Current path: Icom UDP Opus → server decode → PCM → WebSocket → browser decode → AudioWorklet. Multiple encode/decode cycles add latency.

WebRTC provides: P2P audio with hardware echo cancellation, adaptive jitter buffer, automatic bitrate adjustment, and native browser support. Expected 3-5x latency reduction.

docs.title: Current Architecture
docs.text:
Radio → UDP Opus → Server → PCM → WS binary → Browser AudioWorklet
Browser Mic → WS binary → Server → Opus encode → UDP → Radio

docs.title: Proposed Architecture
docs.text:
Radio → UDP Opus → Server (aiortc) ← WebRTC → Browser
Browser Mic → WebRTC → Server (aiortc) → Opus → UDP → Radio

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Add aiortc dependency (Python WebRTC)
  • [ ] WebRTC signaling endpoint (/api/v1/rtc/offer, /api/v1/rtc/answer)
  • [ ] Server-side: bridge Icom audio ↔ WebRTC audio track
  • [ ] Client-side: RTCPeerConnection replacing AudioWorklet WS consumer
  • [ ] Fallback: keep WS audio for browsers without WebRTC
  • [ ] TX audio: microphone → WebRTC → server → Icom TX
  • [ ] Measure and compare latency (before/after)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• aiortc is pure Python WebRTC — no native dependencies
  • ICE/STUN not needed for same-network (direct connection)
  • For remote access via VPN: TURN server may be needed

docs.title: ✅ #105: Built-in FT8/FT4 decoder and auto-QSO
docs.text: Labels: enhancement

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Integrate FT8/FT4 decoding directly into the web UI — decode signals from the radio audio stream, display decoded messages, and enable one-click QSO.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text: Current workflow: WSJT-X → flrig → rigctld → radio. Four separate programs with complex configuration. If the decoder is built into our web UI, it's zero-config and works remotely from any browser.

docs.title: Design
docs.text:
Audio RX → FFT → FT8 decode → Message table → Click → Auto TX sequence
                                    ↓
                              Waterfall overlay (signal markers)

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• [ ] FT8 decoder (Python port or ft8_lib / wsjtx-utils bindings)
  • [ ] FT4 decoder
  • [ ] Decoded messages table in UI (callsign, grid, SNR, freq offset)
  • [ ] Waterfall overlay: decoded signal markers
  • [ ] Click-to-call: select a station → auto-fill TX message → QSO sequence
  • [ ] TX message generation (CQ, report, RR73)
  • [ ] Audio TX injection: generate FT8 tones → radio TX audio
  • [ ] Auto-sequencing: full QSO without manual intervention
  • [ ] Frequency calibration: sync with radio's exact frequency
  • [ ] Integration with logging (#TBD)

docs.title: Technical Notes
docs.text:
• FT8 decode needs 12.6s audio windows at 48kHz
  • Decode is CPU-bound — consider numpy/numba optimization
  • Existing audio RX stream provides the input
  • TX audio generation: numpy sine wave synthesis → push to radio
  • Reference: WSJT-X source (Fortran), ft8_lib (C), jtdx

docs.title: Ambitious but achievable
docs.text:
• Core FT8 decode: ~500 lines Python with numpy
  • TX tone generation: ~100 lines
  • UI table + waterfall overlay: ~200 lines JS
  • Auto-sequencing: ~300 lines Python state machine

docs.title: ✅ #106: Virtual audio devices — system mic/speaker for WSJT-X, fldigi, etc.
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P1

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Expose IC-7610 LAN audio as standard system audio devices (virtual microphone + virtual speaker). Any software (WSJT-X, fldigi, JS8Call, etc.) can use the radio as a normal sound card — no special configuration.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text:
Currently, digital mode software (WSJT-X, JTDX, JS8Call, fldigi) requires either: - Physical USB cable to the radio, OR - flrig/rigctld + virtual audio cables (VB-Cable, Soundflower, PulseAudio loopback)

This is fragile, OS-specific, and breaks remote operation. If icom-lan creates virtual audio devices that bridge LAN audio ↔ system audio, any software works out of the box — locally or remotely.

docs.title: Design
docs.text:
┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│  IC-7610    │ UDP │  icom-lan    │ sys │  WSJT-X     │
│  LAN audio  │◄───►│  server      │◄───►│  fldigi     │
│  (Opus/PCM) │     │              │     │  JS8Call     │
└─────────────┘     │  Virtual Mic │     │  Any app    │
                    │  Virtual Spk │     └─────────────┘
                    └──────────────┘
  • Virtual Microphone (output device from our perspective): radio RX audio → system audio input. WSJT-X sees it as "icom-lan RX"
  • Virtual Speaker (input device from our perspective): system audio output → radio TX audio. WSJT-X sends to "icom-lan TX"

docs.title: Platform Support
docs.text:
Platform Virtual Audio Backend macOS CoreAudio HAL plugin, or BlackHole/Soundflower integration Linux PulseAudio null-sink / PipeWire virtual device Windows Virtual Audio Cable API / WASAPI loopback

Simplest cross-platform: use sounddevice (PortAudio) to write RX to a loopback device and read TX from another.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] RX path: radio Opus/PCM → decode → write to virtual mic device
  • [ ] TX path: read from virtual speaker device → encode → radio TX audio
  • [ ] Auto-create virtual devices on startup (platform-specific)
  • [ ] CLI: icom-lan audio-bridge mode (standalone, no web UI required)
  • [ ] Also integrate into web mode (run alongside web UI)
  • [ ] Sample rate matching: radio 48kHz ↔ device sample rate
  • [ ] Latency monitoring and buffer tuning
  • [ ] Device naming: "icom-lan RX (IC-7610)" / "icom-lan TX (IC-7610)"
  • [ ] Multiple radios: one pair of virtual devices per radio instance

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
1. WSJT-X remote: radio in shack, WSJT-X on laptop, connected via VPN
  2. fldigi/JS8Call: same setup, any digital mode software
  3. Recording: pipe RX audio to Audacity or OBS
  4. Streaming: pipe RX to Discord/Mumble for net operations
  5. Contest logging: N1MM/Log4OM with audio recording

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• sounddevice (PortAudio bindings) — cross-platform audio I/O
  • Platform-specific: BlackHole (macOS), PipeWire (Linux), VB-Cable (Windows)
  • Existing audio RX/TX code in icom-lan already handles Opus encode/decode

docs.title: ⬜ #107: Yaesu CAT backend (FTX-1, FT-710, FT-991A, etc.)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P3

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Add Yaesu CAT protocol backend to support Yaesu transceivers via serial/USB.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text: Yaesu CAT is one of the most common radio control protocols. Supporting it opens icom-lan to a huge range of radios: FTX-1, FT-710, FT-991A, FT-DX10, FT-DX101, FT-891, FT-818, etc.

docs.title: 100 (Abstract Radio interface) — must be implemented first
Yaesu CAT Protocol
docs.text:
• Serial (USB): 4800–38400 baud, 8N2 (most models)
  • Command format: ASCII-based, semicolon-terminated (e.g. FA007074000; = set VFO-A freq)
  • Response format: same ASCII with trailing semicolon
  • Key commands: FA/FB (freq), MD (mode), TX/RX (PTT), SM (S-meter), RM (SWR), AG (AF gain)
  • No audio over CAT — use USB audio device or virtual audio (#106)
  • No scope data over CAT — would need SDR or dedicated scope feed

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Depends on #100
  • [ ] YaesuCatTransport — serial async transport
  • [ ] YaesuRadio implementing abstract Radio protocol
  • [ ] CAT command builder/parser
  • [ ] Radio model profiles (FTX-1, FT-710, etc. — command variations)
  • [ ] Polling loop for S-meter, frequency tracking
  • [ ] CLI: icom-lan yaesu --port /dev/cu.usbserial-XXX --model ftx1 web

docs.title: Hardware Available
docs.text: • Yaesu FTX-1 (portable QRP SDR) for testing

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• Yaesu CAT Command Reference (per model)
  • hamlib Yaesu backend source code
  • flrig Yaesu implementation

docs.title: ✅ #108: DX cluster spots on waterfall
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, ui, priority:P3

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Display real-time DX cluster spots as markers/labels directly on the waterfall display. Click a spot to tune the transceiver.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text: DX cluster is the primary way hams discover active stations. Currently requires a separate app (DXSpider telnet client, web cluster). Integrating spots into the waterfall makes them instantly actionable — see a callsign on the waterfall, click, work.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• [ ] Telnet DX cluster client (DXSpider, AR-Cluster, CC Cluster)
  • [ ] WebSocket-based cluster aggregator (optional: web cluster APIs)
  • [ ] Spot markers on waterfall canvas (callsign, frequency, time)
  • [ ] Color coding: new spots (bright), aging spots (dim), worked (green)
  • [ ] Click-to-tune: click spot → set transceiver frequency + mode
  • [ ] Spot filtering: by band, mode, continent, DXCC entity
  • [ ] Sidebar: scrolling spot list with callsign, freq, spotter, time
  • [ ] Self-spot suppression (filter own callsign)
  • [ ] Integration with QRZ.com API (flag, name, DXCC on hover)

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Telnet client: connect to cluster node (e.g. telnet dxc.nc7j.com 7373)
  • [ ] Spot parser: DX de callsign format → structured data
  • [ ] WebSocket broadcast: spots → all connected web clients
  • [ ] Canvas overlay: draw spot markers on waterfall
  • [ ] Click handler: spot click → set_frequency + set_mode
  • [ ] Filter UI: band/mode/continent dropdowns
  • [ ] Persistence: remember cluster server, filters, own callsign

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
dx_cluster:
  server: dxc.nc7j.com
  port: 7373
  callsign: KN4KYD
  filters:
    bands: [40m, 20m, 15m, 10m]
    modes: [CW, SSB, FT8]

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Telnet client (asyncio, stdlib)
  • No external dependencies needed
  • QRZ.com API (optional, needs API key)

docs.title: ⬜ #109: Audio bridge: custom device naming (icom-lan RX/TX)
docs.text:
Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P3

Name virtual audio devices descriptively: "icom-lan RX (IC-7610)" / "icom-lan TX (IC-7610)" instead of generic BlackHole names. Requires CoreAudio HAL plugin or aggregate device with alias.

Split from #106 MVP.

docs.title: ✅ #110: Audio bridge: latency monitoring and stats
docs.text:
Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P2

Add latency measurement and statistics for the audio bridge path: - End-to-end latency (radio → bridge → sounddevice) - Buffer utilization - Expose via /api/v1/bridge endpoint and web UI

Split from #106 MVP.

docs.title: ✅ #111: Audio bridge: standalone CLI mode (without web UI)
docs.text:
Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P3

Add icom-lan audio-bridge subcommand that runs bridge + rigctld without web UI.

icom-lan audio-bridge --rx-device "BlackHole 2ch" --tx-device "BlackHole 16ch"

Useful for headless servers where web UI is not needed.

Split from #106 MVP.

docs.title: ⬜ #112: Audio bridge: Linux/Windows platform support
docs.text:
Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P3

Currently macOS only (BlackHole + CoreAudio). Add support for: - Linux: PipeWire virtual device / PulseAudio null-sink - Windows: VB-Cable / WASAPI loopback

Split from #106 MVP.

docs.title: ✅ #113: feat: Toast/notification system for backend → frontend messages
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P3

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Web UI needs a notification/toast system for backend → frontend messages. Currently there's no way to communicate errors, status changes, or confirmations to the user.

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Connection errors: "Radio disconnected, reconnecting in 60s..."
  • DX cluster status: "Connected to dxc.nc7j.com" / "DX cluster disconnected"
  • Bridge status: "Audio bridge started (BlackHole 2ch → BlackHole 16ch)"
  • Warnings: "civ_port=0, radio session not ready"
  • Confirmations: User-dismissible dialogs ("Are you sure?")

docs.title: Toast Notifications (non-blocking)
docs.text:
• Appear in corner (top-right or bottom-right)
  • Auto-dismiss after timeout (5-10s)
  • Color-coded: 🔵 info, 🟢 success, 🟡 warning, 🔴 error
  • Stack multiple toasts
  • UTF-8 emoji support
  • Smooth slide-in/fade-out animation

docs.title: Modal Notifications (blocking)
docs.text:
• Center screen overlay
  • Require user acknowledgment (OK / Cancel)
  • For critical errors or confirmations

docs.title: Backend API
docs.text:
• WebSocket message type: {"type": "notify", "level": "info|success|warning|error", "title": "...", "body": "...", "modal": false, "timeout": 5000}
  • Python helper: server.notify("Connection lost", level="error", modal=False)

docs.title: Design
docs.text:
• Match existing UI style (dark glass panels, Share Tech Mono font, cyan accents)
  • Beautiful, not intrusive
  • Mobile-friendly (responsive)

docs.title: Technical Notes
docs.text:
• All rendering in index.html (single-file frontend)
  • No framework — vanilla JS + Tailwind CSS
  • Toast container as fixed-position HTML div, not canvas

docs.title: ✅ #114: chore(web): enforce Radio protocol boundaries in web/ (follow-up to #119)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Enforce that web/ package only uses Radio protocol and capability protocols — never IcomRadio directly.

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
from icom_lan.radio import IcomRadio
await radio._send_civ_raw(...)  # bypasses protocol boundary

docs.title: Level 1: Import ban via ruff (CI)
docs.text:
• Added flake8-tidy-imports banned API rule for icom_lan.radio.IcomRadio.
  • Error message: Use radio_protocol.Radio in web/ modules (and capability protocols) instead of IcomRadio.

docs.title: Level 2: mypy strict on web/
docs.text:
• Added mypy --strict src/icom_lan/web CI gate.
  • Updated web typing to protocol-first (Radio + capability protocols).
  • Removed direct typing/import dependency on IcomRadio from web/ modules.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [x] Ruff ban configured for IcomRadio import in web/ boundary checks.
  • [x] Web layer moved to protocol-first typing/capability checks.
  • [x] CI runs ruff check src/icom_lan/web.
  • [x] CI runs mypy --strict src/icom_lan/web.
  • [x] Rule documented in internals docs.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [x] from icom_lan.radio import IcomRadio in any web/*.py file fails CI/lint.
  • [x] All web/ code passes mypy strict with protocol-based types.
  • [x] Rule documented in architecture/contributing docs.

docs.title: ✅ #115: bug: CI-V watchdog enters reconnect loop (freq=0, all clients RECONNECTING)
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P0, ui

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
In production use, icom-lan enters a persistent CI-V recovery loop where backend radio state collapses (freq=0) and all Web clients show RECONNECTING with no waterfall/scope data.

This is server reliability-critical (not UI-specific): reproduces on iPhone Safari and desktop Safari/Chrome.

docs.title: User impact
docs.text:
• Web UI frequently degrades to RECONNECTING
  • No spectrum/waterfall/meter updates
  • Connect button appears ineffective because backend radio state is dead
  • Affects all connected clients simultaneously

docs.title: Evidence (from runtime logs)
docs.text:
Observed sequence repeats:

  1. CI-V stream stalls 

    civ-data-watchdog: no CI-V data for 2.0s, requesting data start (transport rx_count=26, queue=0)

  2. OpenClose recovery fails for 5s 

    civ-data-watchdog: OpenClose failed for 5.1s, triggering soft_reconnect (1/3)

  3. CI-V transport teardown + reconnect 

    force_cleanup_civ: tearing down CI-V unconditionally
Disconnected
UDP connection lost: None
Soft reconnect to 192.168.55.40 (civ=50002)

  4. Still no CI-V data; loop repeats every ~20-25s.

Additionally, /api/v1/state during failure returns zeroed radio values (main.freq: 0), matching UI symptoms.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Reproduced with active web clients on LAN (iPhone + desktop)
  • Reproduced independent of mobile UI branch behavior (backend failure persists)

docs.title: Candidate branches
docs.text: A. WebSocket/JS frontend instability B. Browser-specific network issue C. CI-V transport watchdog/recovery strategy flaw (backend)

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text:
• A: low completeness (cannot explain desktop failures simultaneously)
  • B: low robustness (cross-browser failures observed)
  • C: highest fit with logs (no CI-V data + reconnect loop + zeroed backend state)

Selected path: C (backend CI-V recovery loop).

docs.title: Likely root-cause hypothesis
docs.text:
_civ_data_watchdog_loop escalates to reconnect too aggressively for IC-7610 session-release behavior.

Current constants in src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py: - _OPENCLOSE_DEADLINE = 5.0 - _RECONNECT_PAUSE = 15.0

Known field behavior for IC-7610 LAN sessions requires longer cool-down (often 30–60s) after teardown. Current 15s pause likely causes reconnect attempts while radio session is still not fully releasable, resulting in repeated dead-on-arrival CI-V streams (rx_count barely changes).

docs.title: Secondary contributing factor
docs.text: After reconnect, web server re-enables scope for active handlers (scope: re-enable queued after reconnect), potentially increasing CI-V pressure during fragile recovery windows.

docs.title: Proposed fix plan
docs.text:
1. Stabilize recovery policy
  2. Increase minimum reconnect pause to >=45s for IC-7610
  3. Add exponential backoff (e.g., 15s -> 30s -> 60s) with cap

  4. Add cooldown gate to avoid immediate repeated reconnect churn

  5. Add explicit recovery states + telemetry

  6. Log/emit state transitions: healthy -> recovering_openclose -> reconnect_wait -> reconnecting -> healthy/failed

  7. Add counters/metrics: consecutive stalls, reconnect attempts, last successful CI-V packet timestamp

  8. Scope re-enable throttling

  9. Delay scope re-enable until CI-V stream health is confirmed for N seconds

  10. Soak test

  11. 60-minute run with 2+ web clients
  12. Pass criteria: no watchdog reconnect loop, non-zero freq/state continuity

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• No recurring OpenClose failed ... triggering soft_reconnect loop under normal load
  • /api/v1/state remains non-zero for freq/mode during client usage
  • UI remains connected and waterfall/spectrum stable on iPhone + desktop for >=60 min

docs.title: Related code
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py (_civ_data_watchdog_loop)
  • src/icom_lan/radio.py (soft_reconnect, _force_cleanup_civ)
  • src/icom_lan/web/server.py (scope re-enable on reconnect)

docs.title: ✅ #116: Backend architecture: Single source of truth for radio connection (radio_ready state) to prevent client-induced reconnect storms
docs.text: Labels: priority:P0

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Currently, front-end clients (both desktop and mobile) can independently trigger radio_connect messages when they detect a stale state or WebSocket disconnect. When the radio's CI-V stream fails and the backend enters a recovery loop, clients bombard the backend with radio_connect and scope_enable commands.

This behavior bypasses backend state management and compounds the load on a failing radio connection, often knocking it into a permanent stale state.

docs.title: Architectural Decision
docs.text:
The connection to the radio must be exclusively managed by the backend.

Clients should never issue radio_connect if the backend has auto_reconnect enabled and is handling recovery itself. The backend should act as the single source of truth for radio health, exposing a radio_ready flag.

docs.title: Proposed Implementation Plan
docs.text:
1. Backend (src/icom_lan/radio.py & src/icom_lan/web/state.py):
  2. Introduce a radio_ready boolean in the state payload (only true when CI-V stream is actively flowing and healthy).

  3. Ignore radio_connect commands from clients if the backend is already in a recovering state or actively managing the connection.

  4. Frontend (src/icom_lan/web/static/index.html):

  5. Remove client-side auto-connect behavior (sendControl({type: 'cmd', name: 'radio_connect'})) when state.radio_ready is available and the backend is handling it.
  6. Bind UI Connect button and auto-reconnect logic solely to the WebSocket connection (controlConnected).
  7. Gate scope_enable requests: do not send them unless state.radio_ready === true.

docs.title: Related Issues
docs.text:
• Relates to #115 (CI-V watchdog loop).
  • Required to stabilize mobile and desktop clients during backend recovery.

docs.title: ✅ #117: bug(transport): fix uint16 sequence wrap-around + retransmit buffer eviction
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P1

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
IcomTransport currently treats packet sequence numbers as monotonically increasing integers, not uint16 counters with wrap-around.

Two concrete defects:

  1. rx_last_seq logic breaks after 65535 -> 0/1 rollover.
  2. Code: src/icom_lan/transport.py (_record_rx_seq)
  3. Uses if seq > self.rx_last_seq + 1 and if seq > self.rx_last_seq.
  4. TX retransmit buffer eviction removes min(seq) instead of oldest-by-send-time.
  5. Code: src/icom_lan/transport.py (_track_sent)
  6. After rollover this removes newer packets instead of truly old packets.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
Under sustained traffic (scope flood + keepalive), 16-bit sequence counters roll quickly. Once wrap happens: - gap detection degrades, - retransmit map quality drops, - long-running session reliability declines.

This is a transport-layer correctness issue, not just observability.

docs.title: Reproduction hints
docs.text:
Minimal check showing stale rx_last_seq after wrap:

from icom_lan.transport import IcomTransport
tr = IcomTransport()
for s in [65534, 65535, 1, 2]:
    tr._record_rx_seq(s)
print(tr.rx_last_seq)  # stays 65535 (incorrect)

docs.title: Proposed solution
docs.text:
1. Introduce modulo-aware sequence comparison helpers:
  2. seq_diff = (new - old) & 0xFFFF
  3. treat 0 as duplicate;
  4. treat 1..32767 as forward progress;
  5. treat >32767 as out-of-order/old.
  6. Rework _record_rx_seq using modulo math for gap detection and forward update.
  7. Rework TX buffer retention to true FIFO by send order:
  8. maintain insertion order (OrderedDict or separate queue),
  9. evict oldest inserted packet, not lowest sequence key.
  10. Add targeted tests:
  11. wrap-around happy path (65535 -> 0 -> 1),
  12. retransmit buffer eviction across rollover,
  13. no false large-gap resets at rollover.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Sequence tracking remains correct across multiple rollovers.
  • [ ] Retransmit buffer evicts oldest-by-time, independent of seq key value.
  • [ ] New tests fail on current code and pass with fix.
  • [ ] No regressions in existing transport tests.

docs.title: Related
docs.text: • Related reliability context: #115

docs.title: ✅ #118: bug(civ): preserve orphan ACKs (ACK backlog) to prevent strict-path timeouts
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P1

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: ACK/NAK handling can drop valid ACKs when there is no active ACK waiter at the exact moment the frame is routed.

docs.title: Current behavior
docs.text:
• CivRequestTracker.resolve() returns False for ACK/NAK if _ack_waiters is empty.
  • ACK is effectively discarded (no short-lived backlog).
  • In strict command paths this can surface as CI-V response timed out.

Code references: - src/icom_lan/civ.py (resolve, ACK branch) - src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py (_execute_civ_raw, strict wait path)

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
This creates ACK timing fragility and contributes to strict-mode instability under bursty/queued conditions.

Observed failure signature (tests and local repro): - test_enable_scope_strict_sends_and_acks times out waiting for ACK, - despite ACK frames being present on the CI-V RX path.

docs.title: Reproduction context
docs.text: A minimal mocked flow where ACKs are present but one gets consumed before next waiter registration can trigger timeout in strict path.

docs.title: Proposed solution
docs.text:
1. Add bounded ACK backlog (deque + TTL, small size e.g. 8-32) in request tracker.
  2. On incoming ACK/NAK with no waiter:
  3. enqueue as pending ACK event (instead of dropping).
  4. On register_ack(wait=True):
  5. first attempt to consume matching cached ACK from backlog.
  6. Keep existing ACK sink behavior for fire-and-forget commands.
  7. Add instrumentation counters:
  8. ack_backlog_hits, ack_backlog_drops, ack_orphans.
  9. Strengthen tests:
  10. ACK arrives slightly before waiter,
  11. ACK bursts do not break strict sequence,
  12. stale backlog ACK expires correctly.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Strict paths no longer time out in ACK-arrival races.
  • [ ] Existing fire-and-forget ACK sink semantics remain intact.
  • [ ] tests/test_radio_coverage.py::test_enable_scope_strict_sends_and_acks stable.
  • [ ] New ACK backlog tests added.

docs.title: Related
docs.text: • Likely contributor to reliability incidents tracked in #115.

docs.title: ✅ #119: feat(core): introduce profile-driven radio abstraction for true multi-model reliability
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
icom-lan declares multi-model support, but runtime behavior is still too IC-7610-centric in several paths.

Examples: - model/capabilities behavior can be too static. - Some routing/framing paths still assume IC-7610 command patterns. - Consumer layers need capability-driven behavior, not model assumptions.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: Without robust profile-driven abstraction: - capability reporting can be wrong, - unsupported commands may be sent, - web/rigctld/CLI paths become brittle for new backends/models, - future backend expansion slows down.

docs.title: Positioning vs #152
docs.text: This issue remains the broader abstraction umbrella.

docs.title: 152 implements the concrete shared-core + serial architecture for IC-7610 first.
119 remains open until profile-driven behavior is generalized beyond that initial implementation and validated for multi-model operation.
Execution coordination
docs.text:
• Foundation work is tracked in #152 and sub-issues #141-#151.
  • This issue should consume those outputs, not duplicate them.

docs.title: Target Architecture
docs.text: Introduce first-class Radio Profile / Capability Matrix: - model id/name, - supports flags (dual_rx, scope, audio, etc.), - command routing/framing policy (e.g. cmd29 use), - feature constraints and validation rules.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Profile-driven model and capabilities across backends.
  • Capability-driven command routing and guardrails.
  • Consumer contract discipline (web/rigctld/cli use capabilities, not model assumptions).

docs.title: Implementation Checklist
docs.text:
• [x] Align profile model and runtime wiring with outputs from #152.
  • [x] Ensure model and capabilities are runtime/profile-derived, not hardcoded.
  • [x] Make command routing honor profile framing/support constraints.
  • [x] Ensure unsupported operations fail explicitly and predictably.
  • [x] Add tests covering at least one dual-rx and one single-rx profile path.
  • [x] Validate consumer layers operate via capability checks.

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [x] model and capabilities are correct per active backend/model profile.
  • [x] Command29/framing paths are profile-governed.
  • [x] Web/rigctld/CLI pass without IC-7610-only assumptions.
  • [x] Multi-model paths are tested and documented.

docs.title: ✅ #120: bug(commands): replace IndexError with validated ValueError on malformed CI-V payloads
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P2

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Some command parsers raise raw IndexError on malformed/truncated CI-V frames instead of controlled ValueError.

Affected paths: - parse_mode_response (frame.data[0] access without length check) - parse_meter_response (delegates to _level_bcd_decode without length check)

Code: - src/icom_lan/commands.py

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: This is protocol robustness debt: - untyped exceptions leak through API boundaries, - error handling becomes inconsistent, - malformed network input can produce confusing stack traces.

docs.title: Reproduction
docs.text:
from icom_lan.commands import parse_mode_response, parse_meter_response
from icom_lan.types import CivFrame

parse_mode_response(CivFrame(0,0,0x04,data=b""))   # IndexError today
parse_meter_response(CivFrame(0,0,0x15,sub=0x02,data=b""))  # IndexError today

docs.title: Proposed solution
docs.text:
1. Add strict payload length validation before indexing/decoding.
  2. Raise ValueError with actionable context:
  3. expected length,
  4. actual length,
  5. command/subcommand.
  6. Add parser test matrix for short/invalid payloads.
  7. Ensure callers can rely on ValueError contract for parse failures.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] No IndexError escapes from these parsers on malformed input.
  • [ ] ValueError messages include command context.
  • [ ] New negative tests added.
  • [ ] Existing parser tests remain green.

docs.title: ✅ #121: bug(radios): make get_civ_addr() normalization robust for common model name variants
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P2

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
get_civ_addr(model) does not match expected flexible model-name normalization.

Current normalization: - input: model.upper().replace(" ", "") - registry keys remain hyphenated (IC-7610, etc.)

As a result, typical forms like "IC 7610" and "IC7610" fail with KeyError.

Code: - src/icom_lan/radios.py

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: This creates avoidable UX friction and contradicts the intent of tolerant model lookup.

docs.title: Reproduction
docs.text:
get_civ_addr("IC-7610")  # ok
get_civ_addr("ic-7610")  # ok
get_civ_addr("IC 7610")  # KeyError
get_civ_addr("IC7610")   # KeyError

docs.title: Proposed solution
docs.text:
1. Canonicalize both input and registry keys via a shared normalizer:
  2. uppercase,
  3. remove separators (-, space, optionally _).
  4. Build a normalized lookup map once at import time.
  5. Keep existing aliases backward-compatible.
  6. Add tests for common user-entered variants.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] IC-7610, IC 7610, IC7610 all resolve correctly.
  • [ ] Similar normalization works across all known models.
  • [ ] Error message on unknown models remains informative.

docs.title: ✅ #122: enhancement(api): make set_mode(str) case-insensitive with clear validation errors
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
IcomRadio.set_mode() accepts str | Mode, but string mode lookup is case-sensitive (Mode[mode]).

This makes common user input fail: - "usb", "Usb" -> KeyError

Code: - src/icom_lan/radio.py

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: API is otherwise high-level and ergonomic; strict case requirement is unnecessary and error-prone.

docs.title: Proposed solution
docs.text:
1. Normalize string input before enum lookup:
  2. mode_key = mode.strip().upper()
  3. Convert lookup errors into consistent ValueError/CommandError with supported values list.
  4. Add tests for mixed-case and whitespace-padded inputs.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] set_mode("USB"), set_mode("usb"), set_mode(" Usb ") behave equivalently.
  • [ ] Invalid mode names return clear user-facing error.
  • [ ] Existing enum-based usage is unchanged.

docs.title: ✅ #123: docs: eliminate docs/runtime drift and add reliability-focused docs sync gate
docs.text: Labels: documentation, area:docs, priority:P1

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Project documentation has drift from current code/runtime state.

Examples observed: 1. docs/PROJECT.md still reports older release/test counters. 2. docs/guide/configuration.md documents ICOM_STRICT_FRONTEND, but codebase does not currently consume this flag. 3. Stability-critical behavior and known caveats are not centralized in a single operational reliability section.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: When docs lag behind runtime reality: - users and contributors make wrong assumptions, - operational debugging is slower, - roadmap/prioritization discussions get noisy.

docs.title: Proposed solution
docs.text:
1. Perform a docs integrity pass for reliability-related docs:
  2. docs/PROJECT.md
  3. docs/guide/configuration.md
  4. README reliability/known-issues sections
  5. Either implement documented flags or remove/deprecate documentation for non-existent flags.
  6. Add a lightweight “docs sync checklist” to release workflow:
  7. version,
  8. test counts,
  9. active env flags,
  10. known reliability issues.
  11. Add CI docs check (optional): grep/assertions for critical config keys to avoid stale env var docs.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] PROJECT.md reflects current release/status data.
  • [ ] Config docs match actual supported env vars.
  • [ ] Reliability section links active high-priority reliability issues.
  • [ ] New release checklist includes docs synchronization gate.

docs.title: Related
docs.text: • Reliability focus: #115, #116, #117, #118, #119

docs.title: ✅ #124: bug(control): send token-remove on teardown and handle status error-driven cooldown
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P1

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Control-session teardown does not explicitly release token/session on disconnect/reconnect cleanup.

In current code: - _send_token() supports magic=0x01 (remove), but this path appears unused. - disconnect() / reconnect cleanup currently closes transports without explicit token remove call.

Code refs: - src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py (_send_token, magic semantics) - src/icom_lan/radio.py (disconnect, _reconnect_loop cleanup paths)

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text:
wfview explicitly sends token removal on teardown (sendToken(0x01)), and also treats status error signals (e.g. 0xffffffff) as session-level rejection requiring cooldown.

Reference: - references/wfview/src/radio/icomudphandler.cpp:101-103 (token removal) - references/wfview/src/radio/icomudphandler.cpp:668-686 (sendToken impl) - references/wfview/src/radio/icomudphandler.cpp:315-319 (status connection-failed handling)

Without explicit session release, reconnect attempts can race stale server-side session state and amplify reconnect loops.

docs.title: Proposed solution
docs.text:
1. On intentional full disconnect, send token remove (_send_token(0x01)) before control transport close.
  2. On reconnect fallback paths that fully tear down control session, attempt token remove when feasible.
  3. Parse control status error code (STATUS packet error field) and surface explicit failure reasons/metrics.
  4. Add cooldown policy branch when radio reports session rejection (avoid immediate churn).

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] disconnect() attempts token remove before closing control transport.
  • [ ] reconnect cleanup path attempts token remove when control channel is still valid.
  • [ ] status error codes are logged/observable and influence reconnect cooldown path.
  • [ ] regression tests cover token-remove invocation path and status-error handling.

docs.title: Related
docs.text: • Reliability umbrella: #115

docs.title: ✅ #125: test(integration): reliability regression suite for transport/ACK/session lifecycle
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P0

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Critical reliability paths are still under-covered by real-radio integration tests. We fixed core bugs around sequence wrap-around and ACK backlog, but we do not yet have stable regression tests that prove behavior under long-running and contention scenarios.

docs.title: Scope (from reliability backlog items 1-5)
docs.text:
1. Sequence wrap-around on transport/control paths under live traffic.
  2. ACK tracker mixed wait/non-wait stress with civ_stats verification.
  3. Long-duration session longevity (token renewal + CI-V/audio activity).
  4. Multi-client session contention to validate civ_port=0/session rejection recovery.
  5. radio_ready state-machine transitions around connect/recover/disconnect.

docs.title: Why this matters
docs.text: Without these tests, regressions can reappear silently in exactly the paths that previously caused session degradation, cascading reconnects, and operator-visible outages.

docs.title: Proposed implementation
docs.text:
• Add marker-based integration tests with explicit env gates for destructive/long scenarios.
  • Reuse existing logging helpers for measurable assertions.
  • Use bounded-time stress loops suitable for CI-local runs; keep true longevity via explicit long-run env gate.
  • Validate radio.civ_stats() and radio.radio_ready transitions explicitly.
  • Include two-client contention scenario in one test to validate recovery and isolation.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• New tests are added and documented.
  • Tests pass on IC-7610 lab setup with expected gated skips when env flags are absent.
  • Failures produce actionable diagnostics (stats + timing + state transitions).

docs.title: ✅ #126: test(integration): add roundtrip coverage for control APIs (data/rf/af/squelch/nb/nr/ip+/state)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Integration coverage is focused on core frequency/mode/power flows. Several public control APIs are not protected by real-radio roundtrip tests, which leaves behavior drift undetected.

docs.title: Scope (from backlog items 6-9)
docs.text:
1. DATA mode roundtrip.
  2. RF gain / AF level / squelch roundtrip and range sanity.
  3. NB/NR/IP+/DIGI-SEL toggle and conflict handling checks.
  4. snapshot_state()/restore_state() transactional restore verification.

docs.title: Proposed implementation
docs.text:
• Add integration tests that:
  • capture baseline,
  • apply deterministic changes,
  • verify readback,
  • restore baseline in finally blocks.
  • Keep tests safety-first (no TX required).
  • Add env gate for optional aggressive frontend checks if needed.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• New integration tests cover all methods above.
  • Tests demonstrate successful restore to baseline values.
  • Failures include enough context for immediate repro.

docs.title: ✅ #127: test(integration): cover PCM audio path and scope capture APIs on real radio
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, area:audio, priority:P2

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Current integration tests validate Opus RX/TX but do not validate PCM audio paths or scope capture APIs against real hardware.

docs.title: Scope (from backlog items 10-11)
docs.text:
1. PCM audio RX/TX integration coverage.
  2. Scope enable/capture/disable integration coverage.

docs.title: Proposed implementation
docs.text:
• Add PCM integration tests using codec-backed path:
  • start_audio_rx_pcm / stop_audio_rx_pcm
  • start_audio_tx_pcm / push_audio_tx_pcm / stop_audio_tx_pcm (gated)
  • Add scope integration tests:
  • enable_scope(policy=verify)
  • capture_scope_frames(count>=1)
  • disable_scope
  • Keep TX and long scope capture behind explicit env gates.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• PCM RX validated by receiving decoded frames.
  • PCM TX/full path validated with gate.
  • Scope capture returns at least one frame with valid bins.

docs.title: ✅ #128: test(integration): codify negative auth/connect coverage and migrate legacy script checks
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: We still rely on ad-hoc diagnostic scripts for some integration checks. Negative auth/connect behavior is not codified as marker-based pytest integration tests.

docs.title: Scope (from backlog items 12-13)
docs.text:
1. Negative auth/connect scenarios (bad credentials, host unreachable/session denied).
  2. Convert legacy integration scripts to pytest tests (or retire with clear replacement).

docs.title: Proposed implementation

  • invalid password/username
  • unreachable host/port timeout behavior
  • expected exception classes/messages
  • Migrate tests/integration/test_connect.py and test_get_freq.py script logic into pytest modules (or deprecate scripts with references).

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:

  • Legacy scripts are no longer the only validation path.
  • Documentation updated with new env vars and test targets.

docs.title: ✅ #130: feat(civ): IC-7610 DSP level controls parity (APF/NR/PBT/NB/filter/AF-mute)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Implement IC-7610 command parity for level-style DSP controls currently missing from icom-lan.

Priority: P0 Complexity: L

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
icom-lan already supports base controls (freq/mode/power/AF/RF/NB/NR/IP+/scope core), but IC-7610 has a broader CI-V command surface that wfview supports.

This issue covers missing level commands in 0x14, 0x16, and 0x1A groups that are required for full operator control parity.

docs.title: Reference (wfview)
docs.text:
• IC-7610 command matrix: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig
  • CI-V command handling: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp
  • Commander interface: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/icomcommander.h

docs.title: Current icom-lan extension points
docs.text:
• CI-V builders/parsers: src/icom_lan/commands.py
  • High-level API: src/icom_lan/radio.py
  • CI-V RX/state projection: src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py
  • Web polling/query model: src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py

docs.title: In scope commands
docs.text: From IC-7610.rig: - APF Type Level - NR Level - PBT Inner - PBT Outer - NB Level - DIGI-SEL Shift - AGC Time Constant - Filter Shape - SSB TX Bandwidth - AF Mute Status

docs.title: Proposed implementation notes
docs.text:
• Add command builders in commands.py with strict range validation and typed docs.
  • Add IcomRadio async get/set methods (Pythonic naming, typed returns).
  • Parse unsolicited/response frames in _civ_rx.py and update RadioState + StateCache where applicable.
  • Keep command encoding generic where possible to reduce one-off helpers.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Every in-scope command has tested builder(s) in commands.py.
  • [ ] High-level methods are exposed on IcomRadio with docstrings and type hints.
  • [ ] RX parser updates local state for all readable commands.
  • [ ] Unit tests cover valid/invalid ranges and ACK/NAK behavior.
  • [ ] Integration tests (marked) verify at least one read/write roundtrip per command family.

docs.title: Out of scope
docs.text:
• Memory channel commands.
  • Repeater/tone commands.
  • RIT/XIT/TX freq status commands.

docs.text:

  • Memory parsing/format context: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp
  • Shared type definitions: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/wfviewtypes.h

docs.text:

  • Integration test scaffolding: tests/integration/

docs.title: In scope commands
docs.text:
• Memory Mode
  • Memory Write
  • Memory to VFO
  • Memory Clear
  • Memory Contents
  • Band Stacking Reg

docs.title: Proposed implementation notes
docs.text:
• Introduce typed dataclasses for memory channel payloads.
  • Keep API ergonomic for library users: one-line read/write operations.
  • Add strict parsing with explicit errors on malformed payloads.
  • Consider phased rollout: read-only first, then write/clear.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Typed models for memory entities are added.
  • [ ] Read memory contents works and is covered by tests.
  • [ ] Write/clear/to-vfo behavior works with validation and safety checks.
  • [ ] Band stacking register read/write is implemented.
  • [ ] Integration tests demonstrate no session instability under repeated memory ops.

docs.title: ✅ #140: epic(civ): IC-7610 full command parity vs wfview (134-command matrix)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P2

docs.title: Epic goal
docs.text:
Reach practical IC-7610 functional parity with wfview command coverage for icom-lan core library API.

Priority: P0 Complexity: XL

docs.title: Baseline (as of 2026-03-05)
docs.text:
Source of truth: wfview IC-7610 profile has 134 commands: - https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig

Current icom-lan status from code audit: - Fully implemented: 38 / 134 - Partial support: 16 / 134 - Missing: 80 / 134

docs.title: Why this epic
docs.text: icom-lan already has strong transport/audio/scope core, but CI-V command parity is fragmented. This epic tracks all missing command families to make library behavior predictable and complete for agent-driven and human-driven automation.

docs.title: Core agent context
docs.text:
When implementing each sub-issue, prefer this layering: 1. commands.py — low-level frame builders/parsers and validation 2. radio.py — typed, Pythonic async API 3. _civ_rx.py — unsolicited + response state projection into cache/state 4. web/radio_poller.py — optional fire-and-forget query/command integration 5. tests/ + docs/ — parity gate and user-facing docs

Local implementation files (this repo): - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/commands.py - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/radio.py - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py - https://github.com/morozsm/icom-lan/blob/main/src/icom_lan/types.py

Protocol/behavior references in wfview: - Packet formats: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/packettypes.h - CI-V command handling: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp - LAN transport/auth patterns: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomudpbase.cpp - LAN session/auth handler: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomudphandler.cpp - CI-V over UDP path: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomudpcivdata.cpp

docs.title: 135: missing system/config family (antenna, CI-V options, mod routing, date/time/UTC)
138: expose implemented parity features through API/CLI/Web consistently
139: add CI parity matrix test+docs gate
Coordination with existing roadmap
docs.text:
Related work that should not be duplicated: - #119 (profile-driven radio abstraction) - #129 (integration reliability backlog)

Use this epic for feature parity scope; keep platform-architecture and reliability infra in their dedicated epics.

docs.title: Definition of done
docs.text:
• [ ] All sub-issues merged.
  • [ ] docs/PROJECT.md parity status updated with concrete counts.
  • [ ] Command parity matrix in docs is current and CI-protected.
  • [ ] No regressions in existing transport/audio/scope/rigctld tests.

docs.title: ✅ #141: refactor(core): extract shared Icom7610CoreRadio from current IcomRadio (no behavior changes)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, type:tech-debt, priority:P1

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Extract a shared executable IC-7610 logic core (Icom7610CoreRadio) from the current IcomRadio implementation without behavior changes.

Priority: P0 Complexity: L

docs.title: Why
docs.text: To maximize code reuse for USB backend, we need one shared core for command/state/civ logic, and thin transport/session/audio adapters.

docs.title: Agent Context
docs.text:
Current logic to reuse lives in: - src/icom_lan/radio.py - src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py - src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py - src/icom_lan/commander.py - src/icom_lan/civ.py - src/icom_lan/radio_state.py

Planned target layout (suggested): - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/core.py - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/lan.py (thin wrapper)

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Move backend-agnostic IC-7610 logic into a shared core class.
  • Keep current LAN behavior identical.
  • Keep IcomRadio import and behavior working exactly as today.

docs.title: Out of scope
docs.text:
• Serial transport implementation.
  • USB audio implementation.
  • CLI/Web/rigctld backend switching.

docs.title: Implementation Checklist
docs.text:
• [x] Introduce Icom7610CoreRadio class and move shared fields/methods from IcomRadio.
  • [x] Keep CI-V request tracker, commander, state cache, scope assembly, callbacks in core.
  • [x] Keep LAN-specific handshake/session pieces behind LAN wrapper (Icom7610LanRadio or equivalent).
  • [x] Preserve public API signatures (no breaking changes for existing users).
  • [x] Preserve IcomRadio as backward-compatible alias/path.
  • [x] Ensure all existing LAN tests still pass with no expectation updates.

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [x] Existing LAN unit/integration tests pass unchanged.
  • [x] IcomRadio user code continues to work without migration.
  • [x] No duplication introduced between core and LAN wrapper.
  • [x] New architecture supports plugging non-LAN drivers into same core.

docs.title: Risks / Notes
docs.text:
• Large refactor risk: use small commits and mechanical extraction first.
  • Do not change behavior in this issue; this is structural only.

Add backend driver contracts (CivLink, SessionDriver, AudioDriver) and a factory/config layer (create_radio) to assemble IC-7610 LAN/Serial backends around shared core.

Current entry points to migrate gradually: - src/icom_lan/cli.py - src/icom_lan/web/server.py - src/icom_lan/rigctld/* - src/icom_lan/sync.py

Suggested files: - src/icom_lan/backends/config.py - src/icom_lan/backends/factory.py - src/icom_lan/backends/icom7610/drivers/contracts.py

docs.text:

  • Define typed backend config models (lan/serial).

  • Keep backward compatibility (IcomRadio alias to LAN backend path).

docs.title: Out of scope

  • USB audio engine internals.
  • Full consumer migration (handled in dedicated issues).

docs.title: Implementation Checklist
docs.text:

  • [x] Add internal driver contracts for CI-V link, session strategy, and audio path.
  • [x] Implement factory creation for LAN backend (serial stub allowed initially).
  • [x] Keep old constructors/import paths operational (IcomRadio compatibility).
  • [x] Add smoke tests for factory + config validation.

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:

  • [x] Invalid backend/config combinations fail with actionable errors.
  • [x] IcomRadio remains source-compatible for existing users.

docs.title: ✅ #143: feat(serial): implement SerialCivLink for IC-7610 USB CI-V (framing, health, reconnect guardrails)

docs.title: ✅ #152: epic(backends): IC-7610 shared core + USB serial/audio backend (LAN-compatible architecture)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, type:feature, priority:P0

docs.title: Epic Goal
docs.text:
Implement IC-7610 USB backend (CI-V over serial + USB audio devices) with maximum code reuse via a shared IC-7610 executable core and thin LAN/Serial adapters.

Priority: P0 Complexity: XL

docs.title: Problem Statement
docs.text: Current multi-backend abstraction is mostly contractual (Protocol), while implementation is LAN-centric. We need a reusable IC-7610 core that allows serial backend delivery without duplicating command/state logic.

docs.title: Guiding Constraints
docs.text:
• Keep radio_protocol.Radio + optional capability protocols as consumer contract.
  • No behavior regressions for current LAN users.
  • IcomRadio remains backward-compatible alias (LAN path) during migration.
  • macOS-first for serial+USB audio rollout.
  • Optional deps via extras only.

docs.title: Agent Starter Context (Read First)
docs.text:
Core code areas: - src/icom_lan/radio.py - src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py - src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py - src/icom_lan/audio.py - src/icom_lan/audio_bus.py - src/icom_lan/web/* - src/icom_lan/rigctld/*

Existing strategic/related issues: - #119 profile-driven abstraction - #101 legacy serial issue (older assumptions; superseded by this epic architecture) - #114 web protocol boundaries

External references (protocol behavior only, no code copy): - wfview serial comm handler: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/commhandler.cpp - wfview icom command logic: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/src/radio/icomcommander.cpp - wfview IC-7610 command profile: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/rigs/IC-7610.rig - wfview packet structs: https://github.com/wfview/wfview/blob/master/include/packettypes.h

docs.title: Global Definition of Done
docs.text:
• [ ] Shared IC-7610 core is used by both LAN and Serial backends.
  • [ ] IcomRadio remains backward-compatible for existing LAN users.
  • [ ] Unified CLI supports --backend lan|serial with serial/audio options.
  • [ ] Web UI and rigctld run through backend-neutral paths.
  • [ ] Serial backend supports IC-7610 core controls + audio + scope (with guardrails).
  • [ ] LAN regression suite remains green.
  • [ ] Docs reflect actual runtime behavior and migration path.

docs.title: Coordination Notes for Implementing Agents
docs.text:
• Work strictly TDD-first per repo guidance.
  • Prefer small PRs: one sub-issue = one logical unit.
  • Avoid speculative abstraction beyond IC-7610 shared-core boundary.
  • For serial scope throughput, preserve current CI-V drain-all + command-serialization principles.

docs.title: ✅ #153: TX Freq Monitor (0x1C 0x03) not working on IC-7610
docs.text: Labels: bug

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: TX Frequency Monitor command (0x1C 0x03) does not work on IC-7610 hardware.

docs.title: Test results
docs.text:
• SET command: Accepted without error (no NAK)
  • GET command: Always returns False
  • Expected: Should toggle on/off like other features (per wfview matrix)

docs.title: Tested on
docs.text:
• Radio: IC-7610
  • Firmware: 1.42 (latest)
  • Connection: LAN (wfview protocol)

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
GET:  FE FE 98 E0 1C 03 FD
SET ON:  FE FE 98 E0 1C 03 01 FD
SET OFF: FE FE 98 E0 1C 03 00 FD

docs.title: What this command does
docs.text: Based on the 0x1C command family and naming: - TX Freq Monitor: Toggles TX frequency display instead of RX on main display - Use case: During split operation (RX: 14.200 MHz, TX: 14.195 MHz) - Expected behavior: Switch which frequency is shown on main display

docs.title: IC-7610.rig (wfview):
docs.text:
Commands\109\Type=TX Freq
Commands\109\String=\x1c\x03
Commands\109\Min=0
Commands\109\Max=1        ← Should work as 0/1 toggle
Commands\109\SetCommand=true
Commands\109\GetCommand=true

docs.title: IC-7300.rig (comparison):
docs.text:
Commands\159\Max=0        ← Only 0 (read-only or OFF-only)

Note: IC-7610 matrix shows Max=1, but real hardware doesn't respond to SET.

docs.title: 0x1C Command Family
docs.text:
0x1C 0x00 — Transceiver Status
0x1C 0x01 — Tuner/ATU Status (✅ works)
0x1C 0x02 — XFC Status
0x1C 0x03 — TX Freq Monitor (❌ doesn't work)
0x1C 0x04 — CI-V Output (ANT)

docs.title: Hypotheses
docs.text:
1. Not implemented — Icom didn't implement this feature on IC-7610
  2. Always enabled — IC-7610 auto-shows TX freq during split (no toggle needed)
  3. Deprecated — Feature existed in older models, removed in IC-7610
  4. Menu-only — Controlled via radio menu, not CI-V
  5. wfview matrix error — Copied from IC-7300, never tested on real IC-7610

docs.title: Impact
docs.text:
• Low priority (minor display feature)
  • Does not block other development
  • Mock tests pass (integration tests work correctly)
  • Other 3/4 RIT/tuner commands work fine (75% success rate)

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
Not a bug in our code — this is a wfview matrix vs real hardware mismatch.

The command is correctly implemented per IC-7610.rig specification, but the real radio (firmware 1.42) doesn't support SET operation.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• [ ] Ask wfview maintainers if they tested this on real IC-7610
  • [ ] Check if older/newer firmware versions behave differently
  • [ ] Consider marking as read-only or unsupported in our docs
  • [ ] Document workaround (if display toggle needed, use radio menu)

docs.title: ✅ #155: feat: expose antenna tuner (ATU) control via CLI and Web API
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, area:cli, ui

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Expose the antenna tuner (ATU) control through CLI and Web API.

The CI-V layer already has full tuner support — builders (get_tuner_status, set_tuner_status), async radio methods, unsolicited frame handling, RadioState.tuner_status field, and state polling. What's missing is the user-facing integration: a CLI subcommand and REST/WebSocket API endpoints.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text: The IC-7610's built-in ATU is commonly used before every band change. Being able to query tuner status and initiate a tune cycle from the CLI or web UI (without touching the radio front panel) is essential for remote operation workflows — especially combined with icom-lan's existing band/freq/mode commands.

docs.title: Current State
docs.text: Layer Tuner support Status CI-V builders (commands.py) get_tuner_status(), set_tuner_status(0\|1\|2) ✅ Done Radio methods (radio.py) get_tuner_status() → int, set_tuner_status(value) ✅ Done Unsolicited RX (_civ_rx.py) tuner_changed notify + rs.tuner_status update ✅ Done RadioState (radio_state.py) tuner_status: int (0=off, 1=on, 2=tuning) ✅ Done State API (/api/v1/state) Exposed via RadioState.to_dict() ✅ Done CLI subcommand — ❌ Missing Web handlers (WS command) — ❌ Missing REST API endpoint — ❌ Missing

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
icom-lan tuner              # GET → "Tuner: OFF" / "Tuner: ON" / "Tuner: TUNING"
icom-lan tuner on           # SET → enable ATU
icom-lan tuner off          # SET → disable ATU  
icom-lan tuner tune         # SET → initiate tune cycle (value=2)
icom-lan tuner --json       # JSON output: {"tuner_status": 0}

Pattern follows existing dualwatch, att, preamp CLI subcommands.

docs.title: Web API
docs.text:
WebSocket command (via existing _COMMANDS dispatch in handlers.py):

{"type": "command", "id": "1", "name": "get_tuner_status", "params": {}}
→ {"type": "response", "id": "1", "ok": true, "result": {"status": 0}}

{"type": "command", "id": "2", "name": "set_tuner_status", "params": {"value": 2}}
→ {"type": "response", "id": "2", "ok": true, "result": {"value": 2}}

REST endpoint (optional, lower priority):

GET  /api/v1/tuner          → {"status": 0, "label": "OFF"}
POST /api/v1/tuner          → {"action": "tune"} / {"action": "on"} / {"action": "off"}

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
CLI / REST / WebSocket
        │
        ▼
   handlers.py (_COMMANDS dispatch)
        │
        ▼
   radio.get_tuner_status()  /  radio.set_tuner_status(value)
        │
        ▼
   commands.py (CI-V frame builders)
        │
        ▼
   IC-7610 (0x1C 0x01)

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Add CLI subcommand tuner with get/set/tune/--json (in cli.py)
  • [ ] Add "get_tuner_status" and "set_tuner_status" to _COMMANDS frozenset in web/handlers.py
  • [ ] Add WS command dispatch in _enqueue_command() for tuner get/set
  • [ ] (Optional) Add REST endpoint /api/v1/tuner in web/server.py
  • [ ] Add unit tests for CLI subcommand
  • [ ] Add unit tests for WS command handling
  • [ ] Update docs if needed

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] icom-lan tuner shows current tuner status
  • [ ] icom-lan tuner tune initiates a tune cycle
  • [ ] icom-lan tuner on/off enables/disables ATU
  • [ ] icom-lan tuner --json outputs JSON
  • [ ] WebSocket get_tuner_status / set_tuner_status commands work
  • [ ] All existing tests still pass

docs.title: ✅ #156: Epic: Svelte 5 Web UI Rewrite (Desktop + Mobile)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, ui

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Complete rewrite of the web UI from monolithic index.html (2731 lines) to a Svelte 5 + TypeScript component architecture with responsive desktop and mobile layouts.

Architecture document: docs/plans/2026-03-07-ui-architecture.md

docs.title: Key Principles
docs.text:
• Server state = single source of truth (/api/v1/state)
  • Three state layers: server state, pending commands, UI state
  • Framework-agnostic Canvas renderers (spectrum, waterfall)
  • Svelte 5 components with TypeScript and scoped CSS
  • CSS design system with custom properties
  • Multi-radio ready (capabilities-driven UI)

docs.title: Existing Backend (already implemented)
docs.text:
• ✅ /api/v1/capabilities — 15 capability tags, model, modes, filters
  • ✅ /api/v1/state — 124 fields, MAIN+SUB receivers
  • ✅ WebSocket — scope data, DX spots, notifications, commands
  • ✅ Radio Protocol with AudioCapable, ScopeCapable, DualReceiverCapable

docs.title: Sprint 0: Foundation (2-3 days, 2 agents parallel)
docs.text:

  • [ ] #158 — Backend: state revision + updatedAt + info endpoint

docs.title: Framework Decision
docs.text: Option Verdict Vanilla JS modules ❌ Short-term fix, does not scale Svelte 5 + TS ✅ Small runtime, compile-time, reactive, scoped CSS React ❌ Heavy runtime, overkill Preact ❌ Smaller ecosystem

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text:

  • TypeScript (strict)
  • Vite (build + dev server)
  • pnpm (package manager)
  • vitest (unit tests)
  • CSS custom properties (design system)
  • Svelte MCP server (documentation + autofixer)

docs.title: ⬜ #169: epic: Frontend & API audit — 20 issues for universal, reliable, architecturally sound UI
docs.text: Labels: priority:P0, ui, area:web-ui

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Full audit of the Svelte 5 frontend and backend API revealed 20 issues across 4 categories: critical bugs, architectural debt, resilience gaps, and missing features. This epic tracks fixing all of them to achieve a universal (multi-radio), reliable (resilient to disconnects/slowdowns), and architecturally sound web interface.

docs.title: Audit Scope
docs.text:
• ~8,300 lines frontend (60 files: Svelte components, stores, transport, renderers, audio)
  • ~1,200 lines backend API (server.py, handlers.py)
  • Live testing on desktop + iOS Safari

• [ ] #170 — set_squelch command name mismatch → squelch never works
  • [ ] #171 — set_att parameter mismatch (level vs db) → KeyError
  • [ ] #172 — Waterfall double-rendering (duplicate scope subscriptions)
  • [ ] #173 — Command responses not handled → silent failures
  • [ ] #174 — BottomBar audioManager.onChange listener leak

docs.text:

  • [ ] #176 — Reduce WebSocket connections (iOS 6-conn limit)
  • [ ] #177 — Remove Connection: close, add HTTP keepalive
  • [ ] #178 — Eliminate hardcoded IC-7610 — capabilities-driven UI
  • [ ] #1700 — Fix optimistic update frequency snap mismatch

  • [ ] #1702 — PTT button timeout
  • [ ] #1703 — Stale state detection
  • [ ] #1704 — Coordinate Audio/Control WS state
  • [ ] #1705 — App.svelte: limit reload retries

  • [ ] #1707 — Pause RAF loop when tab hidden (Page Visibility)
  • [ ] #1708 — Wire up missing UI panels (Settings, Antenna, Tuner, Split/RIT, AGC)
  • [ ] #1709 — Add basic LAN authentication (token/PIN)

docs.title: ✅ #170: bug: set_squelch command name mismatch — squelch never works
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P0, area:web-ui, area:api

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Frontend sends set_squelch command but backend only accepts set_sql. Squelch control silently fails — the backend returns unknown_command error but frontend doesn't display it (see #SUB4).

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
Command name mismatch between frontend and backend:

Frontend (ws-client.ts:225, _applyOptimistic): 

case 'set_squelch':
  if (typeof params.level === 'number') patchActiveReceiver({ squelch: params.level });

Backend (handlers.py:145, _COMMANDS): 

"set_sql",  # not "set_squelch"

Backend handler (handlers.py:617): 

case "set_sql":
    level = int(params["level"])
    rx = int(params.get("receiver", 0))
    q.put(SetSquelch(level, receiver=rx))

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
Option A (preferred): Add set_squelch as alias in backend _COMMANDS and handler. This is more intuitive and matches the pattern of other commands (set_nb, set_nr, not set_n_b).

Option B: Rename frontend to set_sql. Requires updating ws-client.ts and any component that calls sendCommand('set_squelch', ...).

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — add "set_squelch" to _COMMANDS, add case "set_squelch": (or alias to set_sql)
  • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts:225 — update _applyOptimistic case if Option B

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• [ ] Send set_squelch command from UI — verify radio squelch changes
  • [ ] Verify _applyOptimistic case name matches sent command name
  • [ ] Backend unit test: set_squelch accepted and dispatched

docs.title: ✅ #171: bug: set_att parameter mismatch (level vs db) → KeyError
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P0, area:api

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Frontend sends set_att with parameter { level: N } but backend expects { db: N }. Results in KeyError when handler tries int(params["db"]).

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
Frontend (ws-client.ts:231, _applyOptimistic): 

case 'set_att':
  if (typeof params.level === 'number') patchActiveReceiver({ att: params.level });

Frontend FeatureToggles.svelte currently shows ATT as display-only (no button sends set_att), but the optimistic mapping exists and any future UI or external caller would hit this bug.

Backend (handlers.py:620): 

case "set_att":
    db = int(params["db"])  # ← expects "db", not "level"

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
Standardize on level (matches set_af_level, set_rf_gain, set_preamp pattern):

  1. Backend: change params["db"] → params.get("level", params.get("db", 0)) (backward-compat)
  2. Or frontend: change optimistic case to use params.db

Preferred: Backend accepts both, prefers level.

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py:620 — case "set_att": parameter name
  • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts:231 — _applyOptimistic set_att case

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• [ ] Send set_att with { level: 20 } — no error
  • [ ] Send set_att with { db: 20 } — backward compat
  • [ ] Optimistic update shows correct ATT value immediately

docs.title: ✅ #172: bug: Waterfall double-rendering from duplicate scope subscriptions
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P0, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Waterfall scrolls at 2x normal speed because each scope frame is rendered twice.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
WaterfallCanvas.svelte receives scope data via TWO independent paths simultaneously:

Path 1 — Direct WS subscription (WaterfallCanvas.svelte:54-62): 

onMount(() => {
  const scopeCh = getChannel('scope');
  const unsubBinary = scopeCh.onBinary((buf: ArrayBuffer) => {
    // ...parses frame...
    renderer.pushRow(new Uint8Array(buf, 16, pxCount));  // ← renders row
  });

Path 2 — Parent callback (SpectrumPanel.svelte:113): 

waterfallPush?.(frame.pixels);  // calls directPush() → renderer.pushRow()

Both paths call renderer.pushRow() for every frame → each row rendered twice → 2x scroll speed.

Similarly, SpectrumCanvas.svelte has a direct WS subscription (onMount line 43) AND receives data prop from SpectrumPanel. The RAF loop checks latestPixels ?? data so it prefers direct WS (no double render), but it's wasteful to maintain both paths.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
Remove direct WS subscriptions from child components. The parent SpectrumPanel.svelte already subscribes and distributes: - Spectrum: via scopePixels prop (Uint8Array change tracking not reliable for RAF, so keep latestPixels in SpectrumCanvas but feed it from parent callback, not direct WS) - Waterfall: via directPush callback

Clean architecture: 

                    SpectrumPanel (WS subscriber)
                    ┌─────────┬──────────────┐
                    │ scopePixels (prop)      │ directPush (callback)
                    ▼                         ▼
              SpectrumCanvas            WaterfallCanvas
              (RAF loop)               (pushRow)

Remove from WaterfallCanvas.svelte: - Lines 54-62: scopeCh.onBinary(...) subscription - Line 52: const scopeCh = getChannel('scope') (if no longer needed)

Remove from SpectrumCanvas.svelte: - Lines 43-52: scopeCh.onBinary(...) subscription - Instead: add onRegisterPush prop (like WaterfallCanvas) or keep using data prop with direct update

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• frontend/src/components/spectrum/WaterfallCanvas.svelte — remove direct WS sub
  • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumCanvas.svelte — remove direct WS sub, use parent callback
  • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte — add spectrum push callback

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• [ ] Waterfall scrolls at correct speed (1 row per scope frame, not 2)
  • [ ] Spectrum still renders smoothly at scope frame rate
  • [ ] No data loss when SpectrumPanel re-mounts

docs.title: ✅ #173: feat: Handle command responses — show errors via Toast
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Backend sends structured response messages for every command ({ type: "response", id: "...", ok: true/false, error: "..." }), but the frontend ignores them entirely. Users never see errors like "unknown command", "no radio connected", "capability missing", etc.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
1. WsIncoming type is incomplete — protocol.ts:15: 

    export type WsIncoming =
  | { type: 'dx_spot'; ... }
  | { type: 'dx_spots'; ... }
  | { type: 'notification'; ... }
  | { type: 'ack'; ... }
  | { type: 'error'; ... };
    Missing types: response, hello, state, event.

  2. No response handler — ws-client.ts onMessage handlers only check for dx_spot, dx_spots, notification. The response type is never matched.

  3. Backend response format (handlers.py:488-507): 

    # Success:
{"type": "response", "id": cmd_id, "ok": True, "result": {...}}
# Failure:
{"type": "response", "id": cmd_id, "ok": False, "error": "command_failed", "message": str(exc)}

docs.title: 1. Add missing types to WsIncoming
docs.text:
export type WsIncoming =
  | { type: 'response'; id: string; ok: boolean; result?: Record<string, unknown>; error?: string; message?: string }
  | { type: 'hello'; proto: number; server: string; version: string; radio: string; connected: boolean; capabilities: string[] }
  | { type: 'state'; data: Record<string, unknown> }
  | { type: 'event'; name: string; data: Record<string, unknown> }
  | { type: 'dx_spot'; spot: DxSpot }
  | { type: 'dx_spots'; spots: DxSpot[] }
  | { type: 'notification'; level: string; message: string }
  ;

docs.text:
• Track pending commands by id with callbacks
  • On ok: false — show toast notification with error message
  • On ok: true — optionally confirm optimistic update
  • Add per-command timeout (e.g. 10s)

// On command failure:
showToast({ level: 'error', message: `Command failed: ${response.message}` });
