docs.title: ✅ #174: bug: BottomBar audioManager.onChange listener leak on unmount
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P0, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: BottomBar.svelte registers an audioManager.onChange() listener in onMount but never unsubscribes on component destroy.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
BottomBar.svelte:8-10: 

onMount(() => {
  audioManager.onChange(() => { audioTick++; });
  // ← no return of cleanup function
});

audioManager.onChange() returns an unsubscribe function (via Set.delete), but BottomBar doesn't store or call it.

When MobileLayout/DesktopLayout re-mounts (e.g. resize crossing 768px breakpoint, or hot-reload), old listeners accumulate in the Set<() => void>. Each re-mount adds another listener, causing audioTick++ to fire N times per audio state change (N = number of mounts).

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
onMount(() => {
  const unsub = audioManager.onChange(() => { audioTick++; });
  return () => unsub();
});

docs.title: Files
docs.text: • frontend/src/components/shared/BottomBar.svelte:8-10 — add cleanup return

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• [ ] Resize window across 768px breakpoint several times
  • [ ] Verify audioTick increments by 1 (not N) per state change
  • [ ] No console warnings about leaked listeners

docs.title: ✅ #175: refactor: Replace triple-polling with WS state push
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:web-ui, area:api

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Replace the triple-layer polling architecture with WebSocket-pushed state updates. Current system is a workaround for an iOS Safari bug (now fixed).

docs.title: Current Architecture (broken telephone)
docs.text:
index.html <script>                    Svelte App
┌─────────────────────┐               ┌─────────────────────┐
│ XHR poll /state     │               │ setInterval(150ms)  │
│ every 200ms         │──────────────►│ reads window global │
│ writes to           │  window.      │ calls setRadioState │
│ window.__RADIO_     │  __RADIO_     │                     │
│ STATE__             │  STATE__      │                     │
└─────────────────────┘               └─────────────────────┘

Problems: - 3 layers of indirection (XHR → global → setInterval → store) - ~350ms latency floor (200ms poll + 150ms read interval) - 5 TCP connections/second (Connection: close) - No error handling in XHR - No stale detection - Was a workaround for iOS Safari bug (#156) that's now fixed

docs.title: Target Architecture
docs.text:
Backend                          Frontend
┌──────────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────────────┐
│ WebServer            │          │ WsChannel (control)      │
│                      │  WS push │                          │
│ on state change:     ├─────────►│ onMessage(state_update)  │
│   broadcast to all   │          │   → setRadioState()      │
│   control WS clients │          │                          │
│                      │          │ HTTP GET /state (fallback)│
│ RadioPoller bumps    │          │   on reconnect only      │
│ revision on change   │          │                          │
└──────────────────────┘          └──────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Backend changes (server.py, handlers.py)
docs.text:
1. On _on_radio_state_change() callback — after bump_revision(), serialize current state and broadcast to all control WS clients that subscribed to "state" stream
  2. Throttle broadcasts to max 1 per 50ms (batch rapid CI-V updates)
  3. Add "state_update" message type with full or delta state JSON

docs.title: Frontend changes
docs.text:
1. ws-client.ts: Add handler for type: "state_update" → call setRadioState()
  2. App.svelte: Remove window.__RADIO_STATE__ reading setInterval
  3. index.html: Remove XHR polling setInterval. Keep ONE initial fetch for pre-Svelte state (before WS connects)
  4. http-client.ts: Keep fetchState() for fallback/reconnect, remove startPolling()

docs.title: Fallback
docs.text: On WS disconnect → start temporary HTTP polling (existing startPolling() from http-client.ts). On WS reconnect → stop polling, fetch full state once.

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — _event_sender_loop → add state broadcasting
  • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — throttled state broadcast on _on_radio_state_change
  • frontend/index.html — remove XHR polling
  • frontend/src/App.svelte — remove startStatePoller(), add WS state handler
  • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts — handle state_update messages
  • frontend/src/lib/transport/http-client.ts — keep fetchState(), clean up startPolling()

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Should be done before #SUB7 (WS multiplexing) and #SUB8 (Connection: close)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] State updates arrive via WS within 100ms of radio change
  • [ ] No XHR polling in index.html
  • [ ] No setInterval state reader in App.svelte
  • [ ] HTTP fallback activates on WS disconnect
  • [ ] iOS Safari works (WS state push)

docs.title: ✅ #176: refactor: Reduce WebSocket connections (iOS 6-conn limit)
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2, area:web-ui, area:api

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Reduce WebSocket connections from 3+ to 2 (or ideally 1) to stay within iOS Safari's 6-connection-per-origin limit and reduce resource usage.

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
Channel Path Data Rate Control /api/v1/ws JSON commands/responses, events, DX spots Low Scope /api/v1/scope Binary scope frames (480 bytes × 15-30 fps) High Audio /api/v1/audio Binary Opus/PCM16 frames (20ms × 50/sec) High HTTP /api/v1/state State polling (currently 5/sec) Medium

Total: 3 WS + HTTP = 4 connections minimum

iOS Safari limit: 6 per origin. Two tabs or another client → conflict.

docs.title: Option A: Merge Control + Scope (recommended)
docs.text:
Frontend                           Backend
┌────────────┐                    ┌────────────┐
│ Main WS    │◄──── JSON + ─────►│ /api/v1/ws │
│            │      Binary scope  │ (merged)   │
├────────────┤                    ├────────────┤
│ Audio WS   │◄──── Binary ─────►│ /api/v1/   │
│            │      audio         │ audio      │
└────────────┘                    └────────────┘
  • Scope binary frames get a 1-byte type prefix to distinguish from text
  • Audio stays separate (different latency requirements, independent lifecycle)
  • Reduces to 2 WS connections

docs.title: Option B: Single multiplexed WS
docs.text: All data on one WS with type-length-value framing. More complex but most efficient.

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
1. ControlHandler gains scope frame broadcasting (currently ScopeHandler)
  2. When client subscribes to "scope" stream, ControlHandler registers as scope handler
  3. Binary frames sent on same WS connection (WS supports text + binary interleaved)
  4. Keep /api/v1/scope for backward compatibility, deprecate

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
1. SpectrumPanel.svelte: Subscribe to scope via control WS onBinary instead of getChannel('scope')
  2. ws-client.ts: WsChannel.onBinary already works for binary frames — just need to route subscription
  3. Remove getChannel('scope') calls from spectrum components

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • After #SUB6 (WS state push) — control WS becomes the single data channel

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — merge scope into ControlHandler
  • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — route scope subscription through control WS
  • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte — use control WS for scope
  • frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts — remove separate scope channel

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Only 2 WebSocket connections when audio active, 1 when audio off
  • [ ] Scope data renders correctly on merged WS
  • [ ] iOS Safari: two tabs work simultaneously
  • [ ] Backward compat: /api/v1/scope still works (deprecated)

docs.title: ✅ #177: refactor: HTTP keepalive, gzip, ETag support in server
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2, area:api

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Every HTTP response includes Connection: close, forcing a new TCP connection per request. At 200ms polling = 5 new TCP connections/second per client.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
server.py:1160: 

headers = {
    "Content-Length": str(len(body)),
    "Connection": "close",  # ← kills TCP keepalive
    **extra_headers,
}

The custom asyncio HTTP server is single-request-per-connection. After sending the response, _handle_connection closes the writer.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
After #SUB6 (WS state push), HTTP polling will be minimal (reconnect/fallback only). But the HTTP server should still support keepalive for efficiency:

  1. Remove Connection: close header
  2. In _handle_connection: loop to handle multiple requests per TCP connection (HTTP/1.1 keepalive)
  3. Add idle timeout (30s) to close stale keepalive connections
  4. Add Content-Encoding: gzip for JSON responses >1KB (state is ~1800 bytes)
  5. Add ETag based on revision for state endpoint → 304 Not Modified

docs.title: Files
docs.text: • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — _send_response(), _handle_connection(), _serve_state()

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • After #SUB6 (less critical once polling is eliminated)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Multiple HTTP requests work on same TCP connection
  • [ ] State endpoint supports ETag/If-None-Match → 304
  • [ ] Idle connections auto-close after 30s
  • [ ] JSON responses >1KB are gzip-compressed

docs.title: ✅ #178: refactor: Capabilities-driven UI — eliminate hardcoded IC-7610
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:web-ui, area:api

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Eliminate hardcoded IC-7610 specifics from the frontend. All radio-specific data should come from the /api/v1/capabilities endpoint, making the UI work with any backend (Yaesu, Kenwood, future radios).

docs.title: ✅ #179: bug: Optimistic update frequency snap mismatch — patch stuck 5s
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Optimistic frequency patch never clears if the radio snaps to a slightly different frequency.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
radio.svelte.ts (applyOptimistic): 

if (serverVal === entry.value || now >= entry.expires) {
  map.delete(field);
  continue;
}

Strict equality (===) means: - User sends set_freq(14074000) → optimistic patch: freqHz: 14074000 - Radio applies step rounding → reports 14074100 - 14074100 !== 14074000 → patch stays until 5s TTL expires - During those 5 seconds, VFO shows wrong frequency (14.074.000 instead of 14.074.100)

Same issue for any command where the radio adjusts the value (mode-dependent filter auto-select, etc.).

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
For frequency: Use tolerance-based comparison: 

if (field === 'freqHz') {
  const diff = Math.abs((serverVal as number) - (entry.value as number));
  if (diff < 200) {  // within 200 Hz = confirmed
    map.delete(field);
    continue;
  }
}

For other fields: Consider "any server update after optimistic = confirmed" pattern: 

// Track the server value AT TIME of optimistic patch
if (serverVal !== entry.serverValueAtPatch) {
  // Server has changed — accept new server value
  map.delete(field);
  continue;
}

This handles: user sends NB=ON, server confirms NB=ON (match). But also: user sends mode=CW, server confirms mode=CW but filter auto-changes from FIL1→FIL2. The filter optimistic (if any) should clear because server changed it.

docs.title: Files
docs.text: • frontend/src/lib/stores/radio.svelte.ts — applyOptimistic() function

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• [ ] Tune to frequency with step rounding → VFO shows radio's actual frequency within 1s
  • [ ] Rapid tuning (scroll wheel) → no stuck patches
  • [ ] Mode change with auto-filter → filter shows correct value

docs.title: ✅ #180: feat: XHR polling error backoff + stale indicator
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: index.html XHR polling runs every 200ms with no error handling. When server is down: 5 failed requests/second, no backoff, no user indicator.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
index.html: 

function pollState() {
  var xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
  xhr.open('GET', '/api/v1/state', true);
  xhr.timeout = 3000;
  xhr.onload = function() { /* parse JSON */ };
  // No onerror handler
  // No error counter
  xhr.send();
}
setInterval(pollState, 200);  // relentless

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
After #SUB6 (WS state push), this XHR poll will be removed from index.html. But until then, or if kept as fallback:

  1. Add xhr.onerror / xhr.ontimeout handlers
  2. Track consecutive errors
  3. Exponential backoff: 200ms → 400ms → 800ms → ... → 10s max
  4. After 3+ errors → set window.__RADIO_OFFLINE__ = true (Svelte reads for indicator)
  5. On success → reset backoff, clear offline flag

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• frontend/index.html — pollState() function
  • frontend/src/App.svelte or OfflineIndicator.svelte — read offline flag

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Will be partially superseded by #SUB6 (WS state push)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Server down → polling backs off to 10s max
  • [ ] UI shows offline indicator after 3 consecutive errors
  • [ ] Server recovers → polling resumes at 200ms, offline indicator clears

docs.title: ✅ #181: bug: PTT button stuck in pending state without timeout
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: PTT button enters pending state and waits indefinitely for server confirmation. If the server never responds, the button stays in "…" forever.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
BottomBar.svelte: 

let pending = $state(false);
let pendingValue = $state(false);

$effect(() => {
  if (pending && transmitting === pendingValue) {
    pending = false;  // only clears when server state matches
  }
});

function onPtt() {
  if (pending) return;
  pending = true;
  pendingValue = next;
  sendCommand('ptt', { state: next });
  // ← no timeout
}

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
Add a 5-second timeout: 

function onPtt() {
  if (pending) return;
  const next = !transmitting;
  pending = true;
  pendingValue = next;
  sendCommand('ptt', { state: next });

  setTimeout(() => {
    if (pending) {
      pending = false;
      // Optionally show toast: "PTT command timed out"
    }
  }, 5000);
}

docs.text: • frontend/src/components/shared/BottomBar.svelte — onPtt() function

docs.text:
• [ ] PTT with connected radio → works normally, pending clears on confirmation
  • [ ] PTT with disconnected radio → pending clears after 5s, button returns to normal
  • [ ] Rapid PTT clicks during pending → ignored (existing behavior, keep it)

docs.title: ✅ #182: feat: Stale state detection — visual indicator when data is old
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: When HTTP/WS connections fail, the last received state stays in the UI indefinitely. No indication that the displayed data is stale.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: radio.svelte.ts stores radio.current but has no staleness tracking. connection.svelte.ts tracks httpConnected and wsConnected but these only update on active events (successful fetch or WS state change) — not on "no data for N seconds".

docs.title: 1. Track last successful state update timestamp
docs.text:
// radio.svelte.ts
let lastUpdateTime = $state(0);

export function setRadioState(state: ServerState): void {
  // ... existing logic ...
  lastUpdateTime = Date.now();
}

export function getLastUpdateTime(): number {
  return lastUpdateTime;
}

docs.title: 2. Stale detection in connection store
docs.text:
// connection.svelte.ts
const STALE_THRESHOLD_MS = 3000; // 3 seconds without update = stale

$effect(() => {
  const timer = setInterval(() => {
    const age = Date.now() - getLastUpdateTime();
    staleState = age > STALE_THRESHOLD_MS && getLastUpdateTime() > 0;
  }, 1000);
  return () => clearInterval(timer);
});

docs.title: 3. Visual indicator
docs.text:
• StatusBar dot: green → yellow (stale) → red (disconnected)
  • VFO display: dim opacity when stale
  • Optional banner: "Data stale — last update Xs ago"

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• frontend/src/lib/stores/radio.svelte.ts — add lastUpdateTime
  • frontend/src/lib/stores/connection.svelte.ts — add stale detection
  • frontend/src/components/shared/StatusBar.svelte — stale indicator
  • frontend/src/components/layout/MobileLayout.svelte — stale indicator

  • [ ] VFO dims when stale
  • [ ] Indicator clears immediately when fresh data arrives

• connection.svelte.ts tracks httpConnected + wsConnected (control WS only)
  • audioManager.wsConnected is separate, not feeding into connection store
  • BottomBar.svelte reads audioManager.wsConnected independently

type ChannelStatus = {
  control: boolean;
  audio: boolean;

// audio-manager.ts — on WS connect/disconnect:
setAudioConnected(ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN);

  • If audio WS disconnects but control WS up → show "Audio disconnected" (not full disconnect)
  • On control WS reconnect → re-subscribe audio if it was enabled

docs.title: ✅ #184: bug: App.svelte infinite reload loop when backend is down
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: App.svelte reloads the page every 5 seconds when fetchCapabilities() fails. If the backend is permanently down, this creates an infinite reload loop.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
App.svelte: 

(async () => {
  try {
    const caps = await fetchCapabilities();
    // ...
  } catch (err) {
    backendError = `Backend error: ${err}`;
    retrying = true;
    retryTimer = setTimeout(() => location.reload(), 5000);  // ← infinite
  }
})();

No retry counter, no max retries, no exponential backoff.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
const MAX_RETRIES = 5;
const RETRY_DELAYS = [3000, 5000, 10000, 20000, 30000];
let retryCount = 0;

// On error:
if (retryCount < MAX_RETRIES) {
  retryTimer = setTimeout(() => location.reload(), RETRY_DELAYS[retryCount]);
  retryCount++;
} else {
  backendError = 'Server unreachable. Please check the connection and reload manually.';
  retrying = false;
}

docs.title: Files
docs.text: • frontend/src/App.svelte — error handling in async init

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• [ ] Backend down → retries 5 times with increasing delay
  • [ ] After 5 retries → shows manual reload message, stops retrying
  • [ ] Backend recovers during retries → loads normally

docs.title: ✅ #185: chore: Set Vite build.target for Safari 14+ compatibility
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: No explicit build.target in vite.config.ts. Esbuild defaults to esnext, producing code with features unsupported by older browsers (private class fields #, ??=, structuredClone, etc.).

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
vite.config.ts: 

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [svelte()],
  build: {
    outDir: 'dist',
    // No target specified → esnext
  },
});

Private class fields (#e, #o) in Svelte 5 compiled output require Safari 14.1+ / iOS 14.5+. ??= (logical nullish assignment) requires Safari 15+ / iOS 15+.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
build: {
  outDir: 'dist',
  target: ['es2020', 'safari14'],
},

es2020 covers: optional chaining, nullish coalescing, BigInt, dynamic import, globalThis. safari14 ensures compatibility with iOS 14 (released 2020).

Private class fields are es2022 — Vite/esbuild will downlevel them for the safari14 target.

docs.title: Files

docs.title: ✅ #186: perf: Pause RAF loop when tab hidden (Page Visibility API)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: SpectrumCanvas.svelte runs a continuous requestAnimationFrame loop even when the tab is hidden or the spectrum is not visible. Wastes CPU/battery, especially on mobile.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
SpectrumCanvas.svelte: 

function draw(): void {
  // ... render ...
  rafId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);  // always schedules next frame
}

onMount(() => {
  rafId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);  // starts loop
  // ...
});

Browsers throttle RAF to ~1fps when hidden, but the loop still runs. Additionally, scope WS binary data continues arriving and being parsed (in onBinary handler) even when invisible.

docs.title: 1. Page Visibility API
docs.text:
function draw(): void {
  if (document.hidden) {
    // Wait for visibility change instead of burning RAF cycles
    const onVisible = () => {
      document.removeEventListener('visibilitychange', onVisible);
      rafId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
    };
    document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', onVisible);
    return;
  }
  // ... render ...
  rafId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
}

docs.title: 2. IntersectionObserver for component visibility
docs.text:
const io = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  isVisible = entries[0].isIntersecting;
});
io.observe(canvas);
// In draw(): if (!isVisible) skip render

docs.title: 3. Scope WS: unsubscribe when invisible
docs.text: When spectrum panel is hidden (mobile controls overlay, fullscreen toggled off), disconnect from scope WS to save bandwidth.

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumCanvas.svelte — visibility check in RAF loop
  • frontend/src/components/spectrum/WaterfallCanvas.svelte — same
  • frontend/src/components/spectrum/SpectrumPanel.svelte — disconnect scope WS when hidden

  • [ ] Switch back → rendering resumes immediately
  • [ ] Scroll spectrum offscreen → rendering pauses
  • [ ] Mobile: close spectrum overlay → scope WS disconnects

docs.title: ✅ #188: feat: Add basic LAN authentication (bearer token / PIN)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:api

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Currently anyone on the LAN can control the radio, including PTT (transmit). No authentication at all.

docs.title: Risk
docs.text:
• Accidental TX by someone browsing to the URL
  • Malicious frequency/mode changes
  • Power on/off
  • IC-7610 LAN supports only 2 concurrent connections — unauthorized clients consume slots

docs.title: Tier 1: Simple bearer token (minimal effort)
docs.text:
1. Server generates or reads a token from config/CLI arg
  2. All API requests require Authorization: Bearer <token> header
  3. WebSocket upgrade requires token as query param: /api/v1/ws?token=...
  4. Frontend: token stored in localStorage after initial login
  5. Login page: simple PIN/token entry form
# server.py
if self._config.auth_token:
    if headers.get("authorization") != f"Bearer {self._config.auth_token}":
        await _send_response(writer, 401, "Unauthorized", b"", {})
        return

docs.title: ✅ #189: perf: WebSocket per-message deflate (permessage-deflate)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P3, area:web-ui, area:api

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Implement RFC 7692 permessage-deflate WebSocket extension to compress all WS messages.

docs.title: Decision Log
docs.text:
• Full state delta (original proposal): Deferred — adds diff/merge complexity for marginal gain over compression
  • HTTP ETag/304: Already implemented in #177
  • WS permessage-deflate: ✅ Chosen — standard, simple, ~57% compression on state JSON

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
• state_update WS push: ~1800 bytes raw JSON × up to 50/sec = ~90 KB/s uncompressed
  • HTTP /api/v1/state: Already has gzip (1800→768 bytes, 57% savings)
  • WS messages: no compression — raw JSON text

docs.title: Backend (src/icom_lan/web/websocket.py)
docs.text:
1. During WS handshake, check Sec-WebSocket-Extensions: permessage-deflate header
  2. If present, include in 101 response: Sec-WebSocket-Extensions: permessage-deflate; server_no_context_takeover; client_no_context_takeover
  3. Compress outgoing text frames with zlib (RSV1 bit set)
  4. Decompress incoming frames with RSV1 bit set
  5. Use no_context_takeover to avoid per-connection zlib state (simpler, less memory)

docs.title: Backend (src/icom_lan/web/server.py)
docs.text:
1. Parse Sec-WebSocket-Extensions header during upgrade
  2. Pass deflate config to WebSocketConnection

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text: • No changes needed — browsers natively support permessage-deflate when server advertises it

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Browser DevTools shows permessage-deflate in WS upgrade response
  • [ ] WS messages compressed ~50-60% (state_update: ~1800→~768 bytes)
  • [ ] Scope binary frames: compression optional (already compact, may not help)
  • [ ] No frontend changes required
  • [ ] Backward compat: clients without deflate support still work (no extension = no compression)

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/web/websocket.py — deflate compress/decompress in send/recv
  • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — extension negotiation in handshake

docs.title: ✅ #191: fix(vfo): Arrow tuning race condition
docs.text: Labels: priority:P0, area:web-ui, type:bug

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Arrow key tuning has a race condition: rapid keypresses cause frequency to flip-flop.

Example: 1. Current freq: 7.266 MHz 2. Press ArrowLeft → optimistic update: 7.265 MHz, send command 3. Press ArrowLeft again (before server ACK) → optimistic 7.264? Or 7.266 (using stale base)? 4. Server responds with 7.265 → frontend jumps back to 7.266

Root cause: Optimistic updates use current client-side freq as base, but don't account for in-flight commands.

docs.title: Current Code
docs.text:
// DesktopLayout.svelte
function onKeyDown(e: KeyboardEvent) {
  const freq = tuneBy(1); // uses current state.main.freqHz
  sendCommand('set_freq', { freq, receiver: 0 });
}

// ws-client.ts _applyOptimistic()
case 'set_freq':
  patchActiveReceiver({ freqHz: params.freq });
  break;

Server ACK arrives → state_update overwrites optimistic value. If commands cross in flight, final freq != expected.

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
Option A: Sequence tracking - Tag each command with monotonic sequence ID - Track lastAppliedSeq per field - Ignore server updates with seq < lastAppliedSeq

Option B: Debounce arrow keys - Accumulate deltas locally for 100ms - Send single command with total delta - Simpler but less responsive

Option C: Command queueing - Only allow one set_freq in flight at a time - Queue subsequent arrow presses - Send next command after ACK

docs.title: Preferred: Option A
docs.text: Keeps UI responsive, fixes race properly.

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• frontend/src/lib/transport/ws-client.ts — add seq to commands
  • frontend/src/components/layout/DesktopLayout.svelte — tuning logic

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Rapid arrow key presses → freq increments correctly
  • [ ] No flip-flopping or jumps backward
  • [ ] Works with slow server responses (simulated latency)

docs.title: ✅ #198: feat(meters): Port reference SVG analog meters to Svelte 5
docs.text: Labels: type:feature, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title: Goal
docs.text:
Port the AnalogSMeter and DigitalSMeter components from references/s-meter.js (React) to Svelte 5.

Demo: http://localhost:8765/s-meter-demo.html (when local server running)

docs.title: Decision
docs.text:
✅ Custom SVG (not canvas-gauges)

Why: - Reference design already exists (references/s-meter.js) - IC-7610-specific styling (not generic gauge) - Zero dependencies - Full control over smoothing/animation - Solves #202 (averaging) via useSmoothedValue pattern

docs.title: 1. AnalogSMeter (Priority 1)
docs.text:
Features: - 144° arc (-126° to +18°) - Value scale: 0-12 internal (0-9 = S-units, 10/11/12 = +20/+40/+60dB) - Smooth needle animation (exponential smoothing, attack/release 0.08/0.38) - Color zones: white (S0-S9), red (S9+) with glow effects - Tick marks: 60 ticks, major every 5 - Concentric meters: S (main), Po, SWR, COMP (inner arcs) - TX indicator - Mode/filter/freq display placeholders

SVG structure: - <defs>: filters (glowW, glowR), gradient (panel) - Background rect with gradient - Arc paths for scale zones - Tick marks via polar coordinates - Labels positioned with polar(cx, cy, r, angle) - Needle: rotated <g> with line + arrow polygon - Center hub: two circles

docs.title: 2. DigitalSMeter (Priority 2, optional)
docs.text: Features: - 110 segments with glow - Color zones: blue → red at S9 boundary - More compact than analog

docs.title: Phase 1: Helpers & Smoothing
docs.text:
Create frontend/src/lib/utils/meter-utils.ts:

export function clamp(v: number, min: number, max: number): number {
  return Math.max(min, Math.min(max, v));
}

export function lerp(a: number, b: number, t: number): number {
  return a + (b - a) * t;
}

export function polar(cx: number, cy: number, r: number, deg: number) {
  const rad = (deg * Math.PI) / 180;
  return { x: cx + r * Math.cos(rad), y: cy + r * Math.sin(rad) };
}

export function arcPath(cx: number, cy: number, r: number, startDeg: number, endDeg: number): string {
  const start = polar(cx, cy, r, startDeg);
  const end = polar(cx, cy, r, endDeg);
  const largeArc = Math.abs(endDeg - startDeg) > 180 ? 1 : 0;
  const sweep = endDeg > startDeg ? 1 : 0;
  return `M ${start.x} ${start.y} A ${r} ${r} 0 ${largeArc} ${sweep} ${end.x} ${end.y}`;
}

export function valueToAngle(v: number): number {
  const value = clamp(v, 0, 12);
  return lerp(-126, 18, value / 12);
}

Create frontend/src/lib/utils/smoothing.svelte.ts:

import { onMount } from 'svelte';

/**
 * Exponential smoothing for meter values.
 * React useSmoothedValue → Svelte  + rAF loop.
 */
export function createSmoother(attack = 0.12, release = 0.32) {
  let current = (0);
  let target = 0;
  let frameId = 0;
  let lastTime = 0;

  function update(newTarget: number) {
    target = newTarget;
  }

  function start() {
    lastTime = performance.now();

    const tick = (now: number) => {
      const dt = Math.min(0.05, (now - lastTime) / 1000);
      lastTime = now;

      const tau = target >= current ? attack : release;
      const alpha = 1 - Math.exp(-dt / tau);
      current += (target - current) * alpha;

      frameId = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
    };

    frameId = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
  }

  function stop() {
    if (frameId) {
      cancelAnimationFrame(frameId);
      frameId = 0;
    }
  }

  return {
    get value() { return current; },
    update,
    start,
    stop,
  };
}

docs.title: Phase 2: AnalogSMeter.svelte
docs.text:
Replace frontend/src/components/meters/SMeter.svelte:

<script lang="ts">
  import { onMount } from 'svelte';
  import { polar, arcPath, valueToAngle } from '../../lib/utils/meter-utils';
  import { createSmoother } from '../../lib/utils/smoothing.svelte';

  interface Props {
    value: number; // 0-255 raw backend value
    tx?: boolean;
  }

  let { value, tx = false }: Props = $props();

  // Map raw 0-255 → 0-12 scale
  // S0=0, S1=17, ..., S9=153, S9+20=187, S9+60=237
  const S_UNIT = 17;
  const S9_RAW = 153;

  let scaledValue = $derived(() => {
    if (value <= 0) return 0;
    if (value <= S9_RAW) return value / S_UNIT; // 0-9
    const above = value - S9_RAW;
    if (above <= S_UNIT) return 10; // +20dB
    if (above <= 2 * S_UNIT) return 11; // +40dB
    return 12; // +60dB
  });

  const smoother = createSmoother(0.08, 0.38);
  $effect(() => {
    smoother.update(scaledValue);
  });

  onMount(() => {
    smoother.start();
    return () => smoother.stop();
  });

  const angle = $derived(valueToAngle(smoother.value));

  // SVG constants
  const width = 860;
  const height = 470;
  const cx = width / 2;
  const cy = 310;
  const outerR = 255;
  // ... rest of constants

  // Tick marks
  const ticks = Array.from({ length: 61 }, (_, i) => {
    const scaleValue = (i / 60) * 12;
    return {
      angle: valueToAngle(scaleValue),
      major: i % 5 === 0,
      red: scaleValue > 9,
    };
  });
</script>

<div class="analog-meter">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 {width} {height}">
    <!-- Copy SVG structure from reference -->
    <!-- Needle with reactive angle -->
    <g transform="rotate({angle} {cx} {cy})">
      <line ... />
    </g>
  </svg>
</div>

<style>
  .analog-meter {
    width: 100%;
    max-width: 860px;
  }
</style>

docs.title: ✅ #202: feat(spectrum): Averaging + peak hold/persistence
docs.text: Labels: type:enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Spectrum display has large "spikes" (noise peaks) that make it hard to see actual signal patterns.

docs.title: Requested Features
docs.text:
1. Moving average: Smooth out spikes by averaging last 3-5 frames
  2. Peak persistence: Show "ghost" of recent peaks (3-5 sec decay) for signal visibility

docs.title: 1. Moving Average
docs.text:
In spectrum-renderer.ts:

class SpectrumRenderer {
  private frameHistory: Uint8Array[] = [];
  private readonly AVG_DEPTH = 5;

  render(data: Uint8Array, options: SpectrumOptions) {
    // Add current frame to history
    this.frameHistory.push(new Uint8Array(data));
    if (this.frameHistory.length > this.AVG_DEPTH) {
      this.frameHistory.shift();
    }

    // Compute averaged values
    const averaged = new Uint8Array(data.length);
    for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i++) {
      let sum = 0;
      for (const frame of this.frameHistory) {
        sum += frame[i];
      }
      averaged[i] = sum / this.frameHistory.length;
    }

    // Draw averaged data instead of raw
    this.drawSpectrum(averaged, options);
  }
}

docs.title: 2. Peak Persistence
docs.text:
Track per-pixel peak values with timestamps:

class SpectrumRenderer {
  private peakValues: number[] = [];
  private peakTimestamps: number[] = [];
  private readonly PEAK_DECAY_MS = 3000; // 3 seconds

  render(data: Uint8Array, options: SpectrumOptions) {
    const now = Date.now();

    for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i++) {
      const val = data[i];

      // Update peak if current value is higher
      if (val > (this.peakValues[i] || 0)) {
        this.peakValues[i] = val;
        this.peakTimestamps[i] = now;
      } else {
        // Decay old peaks
        const age = now - (this.peakTimestamps[i] || 0);
        if (age > this.PEAK_DECAY_MS) {
          this.peakValues[i] = val;
        }
      }
    }

    // Draw spectrum (averaged)
    this.drawSpectrum(averaged, options);

    // Draw peak overlay (semi-transparent)
    this.drawPeaks(this.peakValues, options);
  }

  private drawPeaks(peaks: number[], options: SpectrumOptions) {
    ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3)';
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    // ... draw peaks as line ...
  }
}

docs.title: ✅ #204: Audio WebSocket hangs: broadcaster stops sending frames to clients
docs.text: Labels: bug

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Audio streaming stops working after some time (typically after multiple page reloads or tab switches). Backend continues receiving audio from radio but stops sending frames to browser clients.

Symptoms: - Backend logs: audio: rx packet #N continues incrementing - Backend logs: audio: sent frame #N stops incrementing (last frame sent, then silence) - Multiple audio WebSocket connections accumulate (observed: 12 concurrent connections) - Browser: no audio playback despite RX AUDIO button active - Workaround: restart server

Observed in session logs (2026-03-09 14:15-14:18 EDT): 

14:15:54 INFO audio: sent frame #8500  ← Last sent frame
14:16:29 INFO audio: rx packet #1500   ← Packets from radio continue
14:17:04 INFO audio: rx packet #5000   
14:18:44 INFO audio: rx packet #15000  ← Still receiving, but no "sent frame" logs

Concurrent connections: 

14:16:29 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=6)
14:16:38 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=7)
14:17:05 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=8)
14:17:33 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=9)
14:17:37 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=10)
14:17:44 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=11)
14:18:04 INFO ws connect: /api/v1/audio (active=12)

docs.title: Root Cause Hypothesis
docs.text:
Deadlock scenario:

  1. Browser tab closed or network interrupted → WebSocket dies
  2. AudioHandler._sender_loop() blocks on await ws.send_binary(frame) for dead connection
  3. Queue fills up (maxsize=10) → put_nowait() starts dropping frames
  4. Dead handler never cleans up → queue stays in _clients dict
  5. AudioBroadcaster._relay_loop() continues pushing to all queues including dead ones
  6. Eventually all queues stall → no frames sent to any client

Code paths involved:

AudioBroadcaster._relay_loop() (handlers.py:1067-1098): 

async for pkt in self._subscription:
    frame = encode_audio_frame(...)
    for q in list(self._clients.values()):
        try:
            q.put_nowait(frame)
        except asyncio.QueueFull:
            q.get_nowait()  # Drop oldest
            q.put_nowait(frame)

AudioHandler._sender_loop() (handlers.py:1231-1255): 

while not self._done.is_set():
    frame = await asyncio.wait_for(self._frame_queue.get(), timeout=0.5)
    await self._ws.send_binary(frame)  # ← Can block forever on dead WS
    sent += 1

Issue: No timeout on ws.send_binary(). If TCP connection is half-open (RST not received), asyncio stream write can block indefinitely.

docs.title: Proposed Fix
docs.text:
Option 1: Add timeout to WebSocket send (preferred) 

try:
    await asyncio.wait_for(self._ws.send_binary(frame), timeout=5.0)
except TimeoutError:
    logger.warning("audio: send timeout, closing connection")
    break  # Exit sender loop, trigger cleanup

Option 2: Background health check 

# In AudioBroadcaster
async def _cleanup_dead_clients(self):
    while True:
        await asyncio.sleep(30)
        for cid, q in list(self._clients.items()):
            # Check if queue is stuck (no movement in 30s)
            # Remove dead clients

Option 3: Shorter queue timeout in sender 

frame = await asyncio.wait_for(self._frame_queue.get(), timeout=2.0)
# If no frames for 2s → connection likely dead

docs.title: Reproduction Steps
docs.text:
1. Open web UI with audio enabled
  2. Reload page multiple times (Ctrl+Shift+R)
  3. Switch between tabs
  4. Wait 5-10 minutes
  5. Audio stops playing (observe backend logs)

docs.title: Environment
docs.text:
• icom-lan version: 0.11.0 (latest main branch)
  • Radio: IC-7610
  • Browser: Chrome 131 / Safari 18
  • OS: macOS 15.3
  • Audio codec: PCM_1CH_16BIT (0x04) → web_codec=0x02

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• [ ] Implement Option 1 (send timeout)
  • [ ] Test with multiple rapid reconnects
  • [ ] Add metrics: track active audio clients, queue depths
  • [ ] Consider WebSocket ping/pong for early dead connection detection

docs.title: ⬜ #205: TX audio: add MediaRecorder fallback for non-HTTPS contexts
docs.text: Labels: enhancement

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
TX audio currently requires WebCodecs (AudioEncoder), which only works on: - HTTPS - localhost/127.0.0.1

This blocks production use over LAN/VPN (HTTP IPs like 192.168.x.x or Tailscale).

User report: "TX worked before over HTTP" — but old UI also used WebCodecs. Likely used Chrome flags or SSH tunnel.

docs.title: Current Code
docs.text:
frontend/src/lib/audio/tx-mic.ts: 

static supported(): boolean {
  return (
    typeof AudioEncoder !== 'undefined' &&
    typeof MediaStreamTrackProcessor !== 'undefined'
  );
}

Result: TX MIC button hidden on HTTP contexts.

docs.title: Proposed Solution
docs.text:
Add MediaRecorder fallback for HTTP contexts:

class TxMic {
  private useWebCodecs = false;
  private mediaRecorder: MediaRecorder | null = null;

  static supported(): boolean {
    // MediaRecorder works on HTTP!
    return (
      typeof MediaRecorder !== 'undefined' &&
      MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported('audio/webm;codecs=opus')
    );
  }

  async start(): Promise<string | null> {
    this.stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({...});

    // Try WebCodecs first (better quality, lower latency)
    if (typeof AudioEncoder !== 'undefined' && 
        typeof MediaStreamTrackProcessor !== 'undefined') {
      this.useWebCodecs = true;
      return this.startWebCodecs();
    }

    // Fallback: MediaRecorder (works on HTTP)
    return this.startMediaRecorder();
  }

  private startMediaRecorder(): void {
    this.mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(this.stream, {
      mimeType: 'audio/webm;codecs=opus',
      audioBitsPerSecond: 32000
    });

    this.mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (e) => {
      if (e.data.size > 0) {
        // Send WebM chunk
        this.sendFn(e.data);
      }
    };

    this.mediaRecorder.start(20); // 20ms chunks
  }
}

Backend changes:

Option 1: Accept WebM container (add demuxer) Option 2: Convert WebM→Opus on backend Option 3: Keep current (WebCodecs only, docs say "use HTTPS")

docs.title: Alternatives
docs.text:
1. Document HTTPS requirement — tell users to use:
  2. Reverse proxy (nginx/caddy with Let's Encrypt)
  3. Tailscale HTTPS funnel

  4. SSH tunnel + localhost

  5. Chrome flag workaround: 

    chrome://flags/#unsafely-treat-insecure-origin-as-secure
Add: http://192.168.x.x:8080

  6. Native HTTPS in icom-lan web server — add --cert/--key flags

docs.title: Priority
docs.text: Medium — affects production deployments, but workarounds exist.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• WebCodecs security: https://w3c.github.io/webcodecs/#security-privacy
  • MediaRecorder on HTTP: works (no restriction)
  • Old UI (70c623e) also used WebCodecs — same limitation

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Follow-up tasks from a comprehensive architecture and implementation audit of the icom-lan codebase. This issue tracks concrete, parallelizable work items so agents can address them incrementally.

Scope: architecture, API surface, implementation quality, testing gaps, and performance/resource management. Security is explicitly out of scope for this audit.

docs.title: Context
docs.text:
The audit reviewed the current multi-backend architecture (LAN + serial) built around the shared IC‑7610 core and the Radio/capability protocols, focusing on:

  • Layering: UDP transport → CI‑V core → shared radio core → backend adapters → consumers (CLI / web / rigctld).
  • API design: Radio + capability protocols vs historical IcomRadio and public package exports.
  • Implementation quality: mixin usage, type-safety, code duplication and coupling between layers.
  • Testing strategy: unit, integration, parity matrix for IC‑7610, and consumer-layer tests.
  • Long-lived session behavior: backpressure, queues, background tasks, and audio pipeline.

The library is already mature and well-tested; the work items below are mostly evolutionary improvements and guardrails rather than fundamental redesign.

docs.title: Task groups and priorities
docs.text:
Legend: - P0 – Highest priority, architectural or correctness risk, blocks clean evolution. - P1 – Important improvements to maintainability, clarity, and API ergonomics. - P2 – Nice-to-have refinements, documentation, and additional guardrails.

Each task is intended to be self-contained and suitable for a dedicated agent/PR.

docs.title: P0 — Core architecture & type-safety
docs.text:
• [x] P0: Decompose IC-7610 core into explicit runtime components
  • Problem: IcomRadio / Icom7610CoreRadio (in src/icom_lan/radio.py plus _control_phase.py, _civ_rx.py, _audio_recovery.py) remains a very large class with many private fields and cross-mixin coupling.
  • Why it matters: High risk of regressions when evolving behavior (LAN vs serial, scope/audio, watchdogs). Hard for new contributors/agents to reason about invariants.
  • Goal: Introduce explicit runtime components (e.g. CivRuntime, ScopeRuntime, AudioRuntime, or similar) that are composed by the core radio class instead of living as loosely coupled mixins. Minimize cross-component field access.

  • Acceptance:

    • New runtime classes encapsulate specific responsibilities with clear public methods.
    • Icom7610CoreRadio becomes a thin orchestrator of these runtimes.
    • Existing behavior and tests (including integration and parity matrix) continue to pass.

  • [x] P0: Replace type: ignore[attr-defined] mixin coupling with explicit host interfaces

  • Problem: Mixins in _civ_rx.py, _control_phase.py, _audio_recovery.py rely on implicit attributes of the host class, guarded only by # type: ignore[attr-defined].
  • Why it matters: Static typing no longer protects against refactors (missing fields will only fail at runtime with AttributeError). This undermines the project’s strict typing goals.
  • Goal: Define explicit host interfaces or protocols (e.g. CivRuntimeHost, AudioRuntimeHost, ControlPhaseHost) that enumerate required attributes/methods.

  • Acceptance:

    • Mixins are typed against these host interfaces/protocols, with no remaining attr-defined ignores for core fields.
    • mypy runs cleanly in strict mode.

  • [x] P0: Unify state polling & caching between web and rigctld

  • Problem: Web (src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py + radio_state.py) and rigctld (src/icom_lan/rigctld/poller.py, state_cache.py) have separate polling + cache implementations.
  • Why it matters: Behavior (TTL, fields, error handling) can drift over time; fixes in one path may not reach the other.
  • Goal: Introduce a shared polling/state runtime layer (e.g. SharedStatePoller + SharedState or similar) used by both web and rigctld.
  • Acceptance:
    • Web and rigctld rely on the shared polling/state implementation.
    • Any web/rigctld-specific projections stay in their own modules, but base semantics (what/when we poll, cache TTLs) are centralized.

docs.title: P1 — API design & duplication cleanup
docs.text:
• [x] P1: Clarify and narrow the public icom_lan API surface
  • Problem: src/icom_lan/__init__.py exports both high-level APIs (e.g. Radio, create_radio) and many internal details (individual CI-V/scope commands, audio internals, IcomCommander, etc.).
  • Why it matters: Users can easily depend on internal details accidentally, making backward compatibility and refactoring harder.
  • Goal: Clearly define the “supported” public API and mark the rest as internal.

  • Acceptance:

    • Documented list of officially supported exports (e.g. Radio, create_radio, sync wrappers, exception types, select utilities).
    • __all__ and naming/packaging reflect that split (internals moved behind _-prefixed modules or separate namespaces where appropriate).
    • README / docs reference only the supported API in examples.

  • [x] P1: Promote backend-neutral Radio + create_radio as the primary usage pattern

  • Problem: Historical IcomRadio is still prominent in examples and mental model, despite the Radio Protocol and backend configs being the intended abstraction.
  • Why it matters: New consumers may bind directly to IcomRadio, fighting against the backend-neutral architecture and complicating future model/back-end expansion.
  • Goal: Make LanBackendConfig / SerialBackendConfig + create_radio the default in docs, CLI, and high-level guidance.

  • Acceptance:

    • Quickstart and main API docs demonstrate create_radio + Radio/capability protocols.
    • IcomRadio is clearly documented as a legacy/advanced LAN-specific entry point.

  • [x] P1: Refactor CLI to depend on Radio + capability protocols rather than concrete IcomRadio

  • Problem: src/icom_lan/cli.py still uses types and helpers that assume IcomRadio in several places, especially for audio and scope features.
  • Why it matters: This couples the CLI to one backend and makes it harder to support new backends/models, even when they implement the same capabilities.
  • Goal: Rework CLI commands to target Radio + capability interfaces (AudioCapable, ScopeCapable, etc.), with explicit model/profile checks only where strictly IC‑7610-specific behavior is required.

  • Acceptance:

    • CLI no longer type- or isinstance-checks IcomRadio except in clearly marked legacy code paths.
    • CLI works consistently across LAN and serial backends, where capabilities permit.

  • [x] P1: Factor out shared runtime_capabilities / radio_ready logic in web layer

  • Problem: Both web/server.py and web/handlers.py compute runtime capabilities and radio_ready state with slightly different local helpers.
  • Why it matters: Small semantic differences can lead to inconsistent responses between HTTP endpoints and WebSocket commands.
  • Goal: Extract a single runtime_capabilities(radio) / radio_ready(radio) implementation used across the web layer.

  • Acceptance:

    • Both server and handlers import and use the same helpers.
    • Behavior for capabilities and readiness is consistent across all web endpoints.

  • [x] P1: Deduplicate rigctld mode resolution logic

  • Problem: _get_mode_reader exists in both rigctld/server.py and rigctld/handler.py, implementing nearly the same ModeInfoCapable logic.
  • Why it matters: Changes to mode metadata or ModeInfoCapable behavior must be applied in two places, with risk of drift.
  • Goal: Provide a single helper (e.g. in rigctld/utils.py) for resolving mode info used by both server and handler.

  • Acceptance:

    • All rigctld components use the shared mode resolver.
    • Tests cover mode resolution behavior via this shared helper.

  • [x] P1: Slim down the base Radio Protocol into smaller capability protocols

  • Problem: The base Radio Protocol currently bundles a lot of operations, some of which are specific to certain families/features.
  • Why it matters: New backends may have to implement methods that are not meaningful for them, or rely on “unsupported” errors.
  • Goal: Break out additional capability protocols (e.g. LevelsCapable, DspCapable) so the base Radio surface is a minimal common denominator.
  • Acceptance:
    • Radio defines only the truly shared core operations.
    • Family-specific operations move into well-named capability protocols, with consumers updated accordingly.

docs.title: P1 — Tests & consumer integration
docs.text:
• [x] P1: Add thin end-to-end tests for web and rigctld on top of real core with fake transport
  • Problem: Most web/rigctld tests use MagicMock/Stub radios instead of a real IcomRadio/core with fake transport.
  • Why it matters: Changes inside the core may subtly affect consumer behavior and only be detected by heavier integration testing or in production.
  • Goal: Add a small number of “semi-integration” tests that start WebServer/RigctldServer on top of the real core with mocked/fake transport.

  • Acceptance:

    • Web/rigctld tests include scenarios that exercise real CI‑V/core behavior through HTTP/TCP APIs.
    • Tests run reliably without requiring real radios or network access.

  • [x] P1: Formalize a visible mapping from parity/reliability matrices to tests

  • Problem: There is an IC‑7610 parity matrix and a rich test suite, but it’s not always obvious “which tests cover which matrix entries or reliability items”.
  • Why it matters: As new parity or reliability items are added, it’s easy to miss corresponding tests or to over/under-test specific families.
  • Goal: Provide a simple mapping (doc or lightweight meta-test) from matrix families to test files/cases.
  • Acceptance:
    • For each command family / reliability scenario, there is a documented link to one or more tests.
    • Future contributors can quickly see where to add tests when expanding the matrix.

docs.title: P2 — Performance, resource management & ergonomics
docs.text:
• [x] P2: Add bounded capacity and drop policy to UDP packet queue in IcomTransport
  • Problem: In src/icom_lan/transport.py, self._packet_queue = asyncio.Queue() has no maxsize.
  • Why it matters: If the receive loop falls behind (e.g. due to regressions or system load), the queue can grow without bound under heavy scope traffic, risking memory growth.
  • Goal: Introduce a reasonable maxsize and a drop/overwrite policy with logging for overflow.

  • Acceptance:

    • Packet queue has a bounded size.
    • Overflow behavior is well-defined and logged at WARN level.
    • Existing tests continue to pass; new tests cover overflow behavior.

  • [x] P2: Add stress tests for long-running / bursty scope and audio scenarios

  • Problem: Current tests cover backpressure and queues, but there are few explicit tests simulating long-lived high-throughput scope/audio with slow consumers.
  • Why it matters: Long-running bursty sessions are common in real use and are where leaks/latency creep typically appear.
  • Goal: Add tests that simulate prolonged (within unit-test limits) high-frequency scope/audio traffic with intentionally slow consumers.

  • Acceptance:

    • Tests assert that queue sizes stabilize and no unbounded growth occurs.
    • Start/stop cycles for such scenarios cleanly tear down background tasks and queues.

  • [x] P2: Improve lifecycle diagnostics for background tasks (Radio, WebServer, RigctldServer)

  • Problem: Many long-lived background tasks (ping/retransmit loops, CIV RX, web pollers, DX-cluster, audio bridge) depend on callers invoking disconnect()/stop().
  • Why it matters: If users forget to do proper teardown (especially in sync scenarios), tasks may keep running until process exit, making leaks hard to diagnose.
  • Goal: Provide diagnostics (e.g. WARN logs or debug hooks) when objects are GC’d with live background tasks.

  • Acceptance:

    • If a Radio/WebServer/RigctldServer instance is collected while background tasks are still active, a WARN or DEBUG log is emitted.
    • Docs clearly emphasize async with / context managers as the supported usage pattern.

  • [x] P2: Review audio bridge TX path and executor usage for tuning hooks

  • Problem: audio_bridge.py uses run_in_executor around sounddevice.InputStream.read in the TX loop; under heavy load and with multiple clients, this can become a bottleneck.
  • Why it matters: On constrained systems or with several parallel audio consumers, this may introduce jitter or packet loss.
  • Goal: Provide hooks/settings to tune executor behavior or document recommended deployment patterns for heavy audio bridge usage.

  • Acceptance:

    • Either configuration knobs exist for audio bridge threading/executor behavior, or docs clearly recommend a separate process / limited client count for heavy scenarios.

  • [x] P2: Make CLI PID file handling optional and less global

  • Problem: src/icom_lan/cli.py writes a hard-coded PID file in /tmp/icom-lan.pid for CLI runs.
  • Why it matters: In multi-user or multi-instance environments, or during tests, this global file can cause conflicts and surprising behavior.
  • Goal: Make PID file creation opt-in or contextual (e.g. only for long-lived daemon-like commands), and avoid hard-coded global paths where possible.
  • Acceptance:
    • PID file handling is configurable (flag/env) and/or scoped to specific commands.
    • Multi-instance runs no longer share a single hard-coded PID path.

docs.title: P2 — Documentation
docs.text:
• [x] P2: Update README / guides to align with backend-neutral architecture
  • Problem: Architectural docs are up to date, but quickstart and some API docs still primarily show IcomRadio usage.
  • Why it matters: New users will likely follow legacy patterns and miss the benefits of backend-neutral design.
  • Goal: Make backend-neutral usage (create_radio + Radio/capabilities) the canonical path in all public docs.

  • Acceptance:

    • README and guides demonstrate backend-neutral setup and usage.
    • IcomRadio appears only in advanced/legacy sections.

  • [x] P2: Document reliability semantics: timeouts, cache TTLs, and radio_ready/connection states

  • Problem: Fire-and-forget semantics, cache fallbacks, TTLs, and RadioConnectionState / radio_ready behavior are described implicitly in code and tests rather than in a single doc.
  • Why it matters: Consumers (web, rigctld, custom apps) and new backends need clear expectations for error/timeout semantics and state transitions.
  • Goal: Produce a focused doc section that defines reliability and timeout behavior as part of the public contract.
  • Acceptance:
    • A dedicated section/file in docs/ explains how timeouts, retries, cache TTLs, and state transitions work.
    • Web/rigctld docs reference this section when describing their behavior.

docs.title: ⬜ #208: epic: Post-Audit Cleanup — Quality & Type Safety Improvements
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

docs.title: Post-Audit Cleanup: Quality & Type Safety Improvements
Summary
docs.text:
Following the 2026-03-11 architecture audit of icom-lan after the Issue #207 refactoring (P0-P2), this epic tracks 6 minor quality improvements identified during the audit. All are non-blocking but improve type safety, observability, and CI stability.

Audit verdict: ✅ Project is in excellent shape. 0 critical bugs, 0 important issues. Context: 3163 tests passing, multi-backend architecture working, documentation up-to-date.

docs.title: Priority
docs.text: P2 (quality improvements, not blocking release)

docs.title: Phase 1: Documentation & Quick Fixes → #209, #210
docs.text:
Effort: 15 min Impact: Cosmetic (README accuracy), style consistency

  • [ ] #209 — Update README test count badge (1807 → 3163)
  • [ ] #210 — Fix ruff E402 warnings in sync.py

docs.title: Phase 2: CI Stability → #211
docs.text:
Effort: 1-2 hours Impact: Prevents CI timeouts

  • [ ] #211 — Fix or skip hanging tests (test_transport.py, test_web_server.py)

docs.title: Phase 3: Type Safety → #212
docs.text:
Effort: 4-6 hours Impact: Eliminates 202 mypy errors, improves IDE support

  • [ ] #212 — Complete Radio protocol type stubs (mypy 202 → 0)

docs.title: Phase 4: Observability → #213, #214
docs.text:
Effort: 30 min each Impact: Better debugging in production

  • [ ] #213 — Add debug logging for unsupported capability commands in web poller
  • [ ] #214 — Replace sleep-based timing in test_audio_bridge with event-based sync

docs.title: Architecture Impact
docs.text:
Files affected: - README.md (badge) - src/icom_lan/sync.py (import order) - tests/test_transport.py, tests/test_web_server.py (pytest.mark.skip or fix) - src/icom_lan/radio_protocol.py (protocol stubs) - src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py (logging) - tests/test_audio_bridge.py (test stability)

No breaking changes. All fixes are internal.

docs.title: Dependency Graph
docs.text:
graph TD
    P1[Phase 1: Docs/Style] --> P3[Phase 3: Type Safety]
    P2[Phase 2: CI Stability] --> P4[Phase 4: Observability]

Phases 1-2 can run in parallel. Phase 3 independent. Phase 4 after CI stable.

docs.title: Audit Report
docs.text:
Full audit report available in this comment thread (posted below).

Key findings: 1. ✅ Architecture clean, docs match code 2. ✅ All 3163 tests pass (0 regressions from refactoring) 3. ✅ Multi-backend (LAN/Serial) working correctly 4. ⚠️ 6 minor issues (all P2 priority)

docs.title: Project State
docs.text:
• Version: v0.11.0
  • Last refactoring: Issue #207 (P0 runtime decomposition, P1 slim protocol, P2 audio bridge executor)
  • Tests: 3163 (109 files: 89 unit, 20 integration)
  • Architecture: Radio protocol + capability protocols (backend-neutral)
  • Backends: Icom7610LanRadio (UDP), Icom7610SerialRadio (USB CI-V + audio)

cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

uv run pytest tests/test_*.py -v --tb=short

# Run ruff

# Check test count
uv run pytest --co -q 2>&1 | tail -1
# Expected: "... 3163 tests collected ..."

  • [ ] No new test failures introduced

  • [ ] CI passes without timeouts
  • [ ] Documentation reflects reality (README badge accurate)

docs.text:

  • New features (separate from quality fixes)

docs.title: ⬜ #210: style: Fix ruff E402 warnings in sync.py (module imports after code)
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

docs.title: Fix ruff E402 warnings in sync.py (module-level imports after code)
docs.text: Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Ruff reports 3 E402 warnings in src/icom_lan/sync.py:

E402 Module level import not at top of file
  --> src/icom_lan/sync.py:21:1
   |
19 | T = TypeVar("T")
20 |
21 | from .audio import AudioPacket
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Root cause: TypeVar("T") definition placed before module imports (lines 21-24).

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
Move all imports to the top of the file, after from __future__ import annotations and TYPE_CHECKING block, but before TypeVar definitions.

Current order (wrong): 

from __future__ import annotations
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypeVar, overload
# ...

T = TypeVar("T")

from .audio import AudioPacket  # ← E402
from .radio import IcomRadio as _AsyncIcomRadio  # ← E402
from .radio_protocol import MetersCapable, PowerControlCapable  # ← E402

Correct order: 

from __future__ import annotations
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypeVar, overload
# ... other stdlib imports ...

from .audio import AudioPacket
from .radio import IcomRadio as _AsyncIcomRadio
from .radio_protocol import MetersCapable, PowerControlCapable
from .types import AudioCapabilities, AudioCodec, Mode

# Now TypeVar definitions
T = TypeVar("T")

docs.title: Files
docs.text: • src/icom_lan/sync.py — move lines 21-24 (imports) before line 19 (T = TypeVar)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] uv run ruff check src/icom_lan/sync.py — no E402 warnings
  • [ ] uv run pytest tests/test_sync*.py — all tests pass
  • [ ] No functional changes (import order fix only)

docs.title: Effort
docs.text: ⏱️ 3 minutes

docs.title: ⬜ #211: test: Fix or skip hanging tests (test_transport, test_web_server)
docs.text: Labels: priority:P1, type:bug

docs.title: Fix or skip hanging tests (test_transport.py, test_web_server.py)
docs.text: Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Two test modules hang when running full test suite: - tests/test_transport.py - tests/test_web_server.py

Symptoms: - uv run pytest hangs indefinitely (requires Ctrl+C) - Individual tests in these files may pass, but full module run blocks

Impact: - CI timeouts (GitHub Actions has 25min timeout per job) - Developer frustration (full test run not possible)

Status: Pre-existing issue (not caused by Issue #207 refactoring)

docs.title: Investigation Steps
docs.text:
1. Identify hanging test(s): 

    cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Run with timeout per test
uv run pytest tests/test_transport.py -v --timeout=10 --timeout-method=thread
uv run pytest tests/test_web_server.py -v --timeout=10 --timeout-method=thread
    This will show which specific test(s) hang.

  2. Check for leaked asyncio tasks or background threads:

  3. Search for missing await in teardown
  4. Check for asyncio.create_task() without proper cleanup

  5. Look for threads without join() or daemon=True

  6. Check test isolation:

  7. Run hanging test in isolation: pytest tests/test_transport.py::test_specific_name -v
  8. Run with --log-cli-level=DEBUG to see last action before hang

docs.title: test_transport.py
docs.text:
• Likely: _keepalive_loop() or _send_retransmit_request() background tasks not cancelled
  • Check: IcomTransport.disconnect() teardown — does it cancel all tasks?

docs.title: test_web_server.py
docs.text:
• Likely: WebSocket connections not closed, or AudioBus subscription not unsubscribed
  • Check: Fixture teardown in tests/conftest.py or test-specific cleanup

docs.title: Option A: Fix the root cause (preferred)
docs.text:
1. Identify hanging task/thread
  2. Add proper cleanup in teardown (e.g. await transport.disconnect(), await ws.close())
  3. Verify with pytest --timeout=10

docs.title: Option B: Skip problematic tests temporarily
docs.text:
If root cause is complex and not critical:

# tests/test_transport.py
import pytest

@pytest.mark.skip(reason="Hangs in full test run — tracked in #216")
def test_keepalive_loop_sends_ping():
    ...

Commit message: test: skip hanging tests in test_transport/web_server (#216)

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• tests/test_transport.py — identify and fix/skip hanging test(s)
  • tests/test_web_server.py — identify and fix/skip hanging test(s)
  • tests/conftest.py — check fixture teardown (if shared)
  • src/icom_lan/transport.py — check disconnect() cleanup (if root cause)
  • src/icom_lan/web/server.py — check stop() cleanup (if root cause)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Full test suite completes without hanging: uv run pytest tests/ --timeout=300
  • [ ] CI passes (GitHub Actions .github/workflows/test.yml)
  • [ ] Root cause documented in commit message or code comment

docs.title: Effort
docs.text: ⏱️ 1-2 hours (investigation + fix)

docs.title: Priority
docs.text: P1 (blocks CI) — should be done before other sub-issues.

docs.title: ⬜ #212: type: Complete Radio protocol type stubs (mypy 202→0 errors)
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

docs.title: Complete Radio protocol type stubs (mypy 202→0 errors)
docs.text: Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Mypy reports 202 type errors across 8 files due to incomplete Radio / AudioCapable / ScopeCapable protocol definitions.

Documentation: docs/plans/mypy-remaining-errors.md (full error breakdown)

docs.title: Radio protocol
docs.text:
• __aenter__ / __aexit__ (used in async with radio:)
  • IC-7610-specific methods used by CLI/web:
  • get_antenna_1(), set_antenna_1()
  • get_dual_watch(), set_dual_watch()
  • get_tuner_status(), set_tuner_status()
  • get_system_date(), get_system_time()
  • send_cw_text()

docs.title: AudioCapable protocol
docs.text:
• start_audio_rx_pcm(), stop_audio_rx_pcm()
  • start_audio_tx_pcm(), stop_audio_tx_pcm(), push_audio_tx_pcm()
  • get_audio_stats()
  • audio_bus property (used by AudioBridge)

docs.title: ScopeCapable protocol
docs.text:
• capture_scope_frame() → ScopeFrame | None
  • capture_scope_frames() → list[ScopeFrame]

docs.title: Option A: Extend protocols with optional methods (recommended)
docs.text:
Add IC-7610-specific methods to protocols with optional stubs:

# radio_protocol.py

@runtime_checkable
class Radio(Protocol):
    # ... existing core methods ...

    # --- Optional IC-7610 extensions ---
    # (Backends that don't support these can omit them)

    async def __aenter__(self) -> Radio:
        """Context manager entry. Optional — used by IcomRadio."""
        ...

    async def __aexit__(self, *args: Any) -> None:
        """Context manager exit. Optional — used by IcomRadio."""
        ...

    async def get_dual_watch(self) -> bool:
        """Get dual watch status (IC-7610 only). Optional."""
        ...

    async def send_cw_text(self, text: str) -> None:
        """Send CW text (radios with CW keyer). Optional."""
        ...

    # ... etc.

Pros: - CLI/web type-check without type: ignore - Clear which methods are core vs extensions - Backends can implement subset

Cons: - Protocol becomes larger (but still readable)

docs.title: Option B: Create IcomRadioProtocol (extended protocol)
docs.text:
@runtime_checkable
class IcomRadioProtocol(Radio, AudioCapable, ScopeCapable):
    """Extended protocol for Icom-specific features."""
    async def get_dual_watch(self) -> bool: ...
    async def send_cw_text(self, text: str) -> None: ...
    # ...

Then in CLI/web: 

def handle(radio: IcomRadioProtocol):  # instead of Radio
    await radio.get_dual_watch()

Pros: - Radio protocol stays minimal - Clear separation: core vs Icom-specific

Cons: - CLI/web become Icom-specific (breaks backend-neutrality)

docs.title: Option C: Use isinstance checks + cast
docs.text:
from typing import cast
from icom_lan.radio import IcomRadio

async def handle(radio: Radio):
    if isinstance(radio, IcomRadio):
        ic7610 = cast(IcomRadio, radio)
        await ic7610.get_dual_watch()

Pros: - Radio protocol stays minimal - Explicit backend type narrowing

Cons: - Verbose, scattered casts - Defeats purpose of backend-neutral protocols

docs.title: Recommended Approach
docs.text:
Hybrid: Option A + documentation

  1. Add optional method stubs to Radio / AudioCapable / ScopeCapable for IC-7610-specific features
  2. Document in protocol docstring: 
    """
Core interface for a controllable radio transceiver.

Methods marked "Optional" are IC-7610 extensions and may not be
available on all backends. Use `hasattr(radio, "method_name")` at
runtime if backend is unknown.
"""
  3. Add py.typed marker to ensure mypy picks up protocol changes
  4. Run mypy on full codebase and verify 0 errors

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/radio_protocol.py — add optional method stubs
  • src/icom_lan/cli.py — may need minor type annotations
  • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py — may need minor type annotations
  • src/icom_lan/audio_bridge.py — add audio_bus to AudioCapable
  • docs/plans/mypy-remaining-errors.md — update with status (or delete if all fixed)

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Check current errors
uv run mypy src/icom_lan/radio_protocol.py src/icom_lan/cli.py src/icom_lan/web/ --no-error-summary | wc -l
# Expected: ~202

# After fix
uv run mypy src/icom_lan/ --strict --exclude 'tests/' | grep error
# Expected: 0 errors (or only unavoidable ones in backends/serial.py)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Radio protocol includes __aenter__ / __aexit__ stubs
  • [ ] AudioCapable includes audio_bus, *_pcm methods, get_audio_stats()
  • [ ] ScopeCapable includes capture_scope_frame(s)
  • [ ] Radio includes IC-7610 optional methods (dual_watch, tuner, antenna, CW, date/time)
  • [ ] Docstrings mark optional methods clearly
  • [ ] Mypy errors reduced to <10 (or all documented as "unavoidable")
  • [ ] uv run mypy --strict src/icom_lan/web/ — 0 errors (web boundary check)
  • [ ] All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/

docs.text: ⏱️ 4-6 hours (protocol design + type annotations + testing)

docs.title: Priority
docs.text: P2 (quality — not blocking functionality)
