docs.text:
• docs/plans/mypy-remaining-errors.md — full error list
  • src/icom_lan/radio_protocol.py — current protocol definitions
  • Python typing docs: https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol

docs.title: ⬜ #213: observability: Add debug logging for unsupported capabilities in web poller
docs.text: Labels: type:enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title: Add debug logging for unsupported capability commands in web poller
docs.text: Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
When the web radio poller processes commands that require optional capabilities (e.g. SetPowerstat, SetRfGain, SetAfLevel, SetSquelch), and the radio does not implement the required protocol, the poller silently skips the command.

Current behavior: 

# web/radio_poller.py (approx line 180-200)
case "SetPowerstat":
    if isinstance(self._radio, PowerControlCapable):
        await self._radio.set_powerstat(data["on"])
    # else: silent no-op ← no feedback

Impact: - When debugging "why isn't my command working?", there's no log entry - User may not realize their radio backend doesn't support the capability - Makes troubleshooting harder in production deployments

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
Add debug-level log when a command is skipped due to missing capability:

# web/radio_poller.py
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ...

case "SetPowerstat":
    if isinstance(self._radio, PowerControlCapable):
        await self._radio.set_powerstat(data["on"])
    else:
        logger.debug(
            "Radio does not support PowerControlCapable, skipping SetPowerstat command"
        )

case "SetRfGain":
    if isinstance(self._radio, LevelsCapable):
        await self._radio.set_rf_gain(data["level"])
    else:
        logger.debug(
            "Radio does not support LevelsCapable, skipping SetRfGain command"
        )

# ... repeat for SetAfLevel, SetSquelch, SetPower, etc.

docs.title: Why debug level?
docs.text:
• Not an error — radio is behaving correctly (doesn't support feature)
  • Not info — would clutter logs in normal operation
  • Debug is appropriate for "this is why nothing happened" troubleshooting

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py — add logger and debug statements

Affected commands (search for isinstance(...Capable)): - SetPowerstat (PowerControlCapable) - SetPower (PowerControlCapable) - SetRfGain (LevelsCapable) - SetAfLevel (LevelsCapable) - SetSquelch (LevelsCapable) - Any other capability-gated commands

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Create a minimal radio mock that doesn't implement LevelsCapable
# (or use a real backend that lacks some capabilities)

# Run web server with debug logging
ICOM_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG uv run icom-lan web --host 192.168.55.40 ...

# Send SetRfGain command from frontend
# Check logs for:
# DEBUG:icom_lan.web.radio_poller:Radio does not support LevelsCapable, skipping SetRfGain command

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Logger imported at top of radio_poller.py
  • [ ] Debug log added for each capability-gated command
  • [ ] Log message format consistent: "Radio does not support {Capability}, skipping {Command} command"
  • [ ] Manual test with debug logging confirms messages appear
  • [ ] All existing tests pass (logging is debug-only, no functional change)

docs.title: Effort
docs.text: ⏱️ 30 minutes

docs.title: Priority
docs.text: P2 (observability improvement — not critical)

docs.title: Reference
docs.text: Code review suggestion: docs/reviews/2026-03-p1-p2-code-review.md section 4 (Minor suggestions)

docs.title: ⬜ #214: test: Replace sleep timing with event sync in test_audio_bridge
docs.text: Labels: area:audio, type:tech-debt, priority:P2

docs.title: Replace sleep-based timing in test_audio_bridge with event-based sync
docs.text: Split from: #213 (Post-Audit Cleanup Epic)

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Test test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor in tests/test_audio_bridge.py uses sleep-based timing to verify that the custom executor is used:

# tests/test_audio_bridge.py (approx line 66-78)
async def test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor(mock_radio):
    custom_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
    bridge = AudioBridge(radio=mock_radio, tx_executor=custom_executor)

    # ... setup ...

    # Start TX and let it run for a bit
    await bridge.start_tx()
    await asyncio.sleep(0.08)  # ← BRITTLE: timing-dependent
    await bridge.stop_tx()

    # Assert executor was used
    mock_run_in_executor.assert_called()
    assert mock_run_in_executor.call_args[0][0] is custom_executor

Why this is brittle: - asyncio.sleep(0.08) assumes TX loop runs and calls executor within 80ms - On slow CI runners or under load, may not be enough time - On fast machines, wastes 80ms per test run - "Flaky test" anti-pattern — depends on wall-clock timing

docs.title: Fix
docs.text: Use event-based synchronization instead of sleep:

docs.title: Option A: Mock with side_effect that sets event
docs.text:
async def test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor(mock_radio):
    custom_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
    bridge = AudioBridge(radio=mock_radio, tx_executor=custom_executor)

    # Event to signal executor was called
    executor_called = asyncio.Event()

    async def mock_run_in_executor_impl(executor, func, *args):
        executor_called.set()  # Signal test to proceed
        return None

    with patch("asyncio.get_event_loop") as mock_loop:
        mock_loop.return_value.run_in_executor.side_effect = mock_run_in_executor_impl

        await bridge.start_tx()

        # Wait for executor to be called (or timeout after 1s)
        await asyncio.wait_for(executor_called.wait(), timeout=1.0)

        await bridge.stop_tx()

        # Assert executor was the custom one
        assert mock_loop.return_value.run_in_executor.call_args[0][0] is custom_executor

docs.title: Option B: Use pytest-asyncio with wait condition
docs.text:
async def test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor(mock_radio):
    custom_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
    bridge = AudioBridge(radio=mock_radio, tx_executor=custom_executor)

    call_count = 0
    executor_used = None

    def capture_executor_call(executor, func, *args):
        nonlocal call_count, executor_used
        call_count += 1
        executor_used = executor
        return None

    with patch("asyncio.get_event_loop") as mock_loop:
        mock_loop.return_value.run_in_executor.side_effect = capture_executor_call

        await bridge.start_tx()

        # Poll until executor called (max 10 iterations × 10ms = 100ms)
        for _ in range(10):
            if call_count > 0:
                break
            await asyncio.sleep(0.01)

        await bridge.stop_tx()

        assert call_count > 0, "Executor was never called"
        assert executor_used is custom_executor

Recommended: Option A (explicit event) — cleaner and faster.

docs.title: Files
docs.text: • tests/test_audio_bridge.py — replace asyncio.sleep(0.08) with event-based sync

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
cd ~/Projects/icom-lan

# Run test 10 times to verify stability
for i in {1..10}; do
    echo "Run $i"
    uv run pytest tests/test_audio_bridge.py::test_bridge_tx_loop_uses_custom_executor -v
done

# All runs should pass without timing failures

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] Test uses asyncio.Event or equivalent for synchronization
  • [ ] No asyncio.sleep() based on arbitrary duration (0.08s)
  • [ ] Test completes faster (typically <10ms instead of 80ms)
  • [ ] Test passes consistently 10/10 times
  • [ ] Same assertion coverage (executor is the custom one)

docs.title: Effort
docs.text: ⏱️ 30 minutes

docs.title: Priority
docs.text: P2 (test quality — not blocking functionality)

docs.title: Reference
docs.text: Code review suggestion: docs/reviews/2026-03-p1-p2-code-review.md section 5 (Positive) — "timing with asyncio.sleep(0.08) is a bit brittle but acceptable"

docs.title: ✅ #215: epic: Post-audit fixes for #207 (6 remaining issues)
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:architecture

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Post-implementation audit of issue #207 revealed 6 remaining issues that were either incompletely addressed or introduced during the refactoring. This epic tracks concrete fixes.

docs.title: Context
docs.text:
Issue #207 defined 16 tasks (P0–P2) for architecture & implementation cleanup. An agent implemented all 16 and marked them complete. A subsequent full audit (2871 unit tests passing, mypy analysis, code review) found:

  • 1 critical bug blocking mock integration tests
  • 2 incomplete P0/P1 items (mixin→runtime migration, mypy clean)
  • 3 cleanup items (dead code, API surface, shared state)

docs.title: Sub-issues
docs.text: # Severity Issue Effort #216 🔴 P0 assert in transport.py breaks mock_integration tests 15 min #217 🟡 P1 Dead code: 611 lines of unused _ControlPhaseMixin + _AudioRecoveryMixin 15 min #218 🟡 P1 _AudioRecoveryMixin still has 24× type: ignore[attr-defined] 1–2 h #219 🟡 P1 mypy reports 197 errors (goal was 0) 3–4 h #220 🟡 P1 __init__.py exports internals, no __all__ 30 min #221 🟢 P2 Shared state polling is TTL-only helper, not full SharedStatePoller 2–3 h

docs.title: Dependency Graph
docs.text:
graph TD
    A["#216 P0: Fix transport assert"] --> B["#217 P1: Remove dead mixins"]
    B --> C["#218 P1: Complete AudioRecovery migration"]
    C --> D["#219 P1: mypy cleanup"]
    D --> E["#220 P1: __init__.py __all__"]
    F["#221 P2: SharedStatePoller"]

Items #216→#217→#218 are sequential. Items #219–#220 can parallel after #218. Item #221 is independent.

docs.title: Key Files
docs.text: File Role src/icom_lan/transport.py:52 Broken assert in _UdpProtocol.connection_made src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py:139-231 Dead _AudioRecoveryMixin with 24× type: ignore src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py:593-1110 Dead _ControlPhaseMixin (518 lines) src/icom_lan/radio.py 99 mypy errors (attr-defined from slim Radio Protocol) src/icom_lan/cli.py 41 mypy errors (calls methods not on Radio Protocol) src/icom_lan/__init__.py Missing __all__, exports 290 lines of internals src/icom_lan/_shared_state_runtime.py Only 48 lines — TTL helper, not full poller

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text:
• No functional changes to radio control behavior
  • No new CI-V commands
  • No web UI changes
  • Security audit (explicitly out of scope per #207)

Follow-up to #207.

docs.title: ✅ #216: bug: assert in transport.py connection_made breaks mock integration tests
docs.text: Labels: priority:P0, type:bug, area:architecture

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: _UdpProtocol.connection_made() in transport.py:52 contains a bare assert isinstance(transport, asyncio.DatagramTransport) that fails when asyncio provides a non-standard transport wrapper (e.g. _SelectorDatagramTransport in loopback/mock scenarios).

docs.title: Steps to Reproduce
docs.text:
uv run pytest tests/test_mock_integration.py -x -q --tb=short

All tests fail with TimeoutError: Radio did not respond to discovery after 10 attempts.

The root cause is visible in captured logs: 

ERROR asyncio:base_events.py:1785 Exception in callback _UdpProtocol.connection_made(...)
AssertionError

docs.title: Expected vs Actual
docs.text:
• Expected: connection_made stores the transport and allows UDP communication
  • Actual: assert raises AssertionError inside asyncio callback → silently swallowed → transport never stored → all sends fail → discovery timeout

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
src/icom_lan/transport.py:52: 

def connection_made(self, transport: asyncio.BaseTransport) -> None:
    assert isinstance(transport, asyncio.DatagramTransport)  # ← THIS
    self._owner._udp_transport = transport

The mock server's UDP transport may not satisfy isinstance(..., asyncio.DatagramTransport) depending on the asyncio event loop implementation. Assertions in asyncio callbacks are silently caught by the event loop error handler and never propagate.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
Replace the assert with a type annotation or soft check:

def connection_made(self, transport: asyncio.BaseTransport) -> None:
    # asyncio guarantees DatagramTransport for DatagramProtocol;
    # cast instead of assert to avoid silent callback failures.
    self._owner._udp_transport = transport  # type: ignore[assignment]

Or, if we want to keep the runtime check:

def connection_made(self, transport: asyncio.BaseTransport) -> None:
    if not isinstance(transport, asyncio.DatagramTransport):
        logger.error("Unexpected transport type: %s", type(transport).__name__)
    self._owner._udp_transport = transport  # type: ignore[assignment]

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/transport.py:52 — remove or soften the assert
  • tests/test_mock_integration.py — verify all tests pass after fix

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• [ ] Fix the assert in connection_made
  • [ ] uv run pytest tests/test_mock_integration.py — all pass
  • [ ] uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration — no regressions (2871 passed baseline)

Part of #215. Follow-up to #207.

docs.title: ✅ #217: refactor: remove dead _ControlPhaseMixin and _AudioRecoveryMixin (611 lines)
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:architecture

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
The P0 runtime decomposition (#207) introduced ControlPhaseRuntime, CivRuntime, and AudioRecoveryRuntime as composed runtime classes. However, the old mixin classes were not removed and remain as dead code:

  • _ControlPhaseMixin in _control_phase.py:593 — 518 lines
  • _AudioRecoveryMixin in _audio_recovery.py:139 — 93 lines

Total: 611 lines of dead code.

docs.title: Evidence
docs.text:
Neither mixin is imported or referenced anywhere:

$ grep -rn "import.*_ControlPhaseMixin\|import.*_AudioRecoveryMixin" src/ tests/
# (no results)

$ grep -rn "_ControlPhaseMixin\|_AudioRecoveryMixin" src/icom_lan/radio.py
src/icom_lan/radio.py:366:    # Watchdog timing (used by _watchdog_loop; were on _ControlPhaseMixin)

  • [ ] Remove any remaining comments referencing the deleted mixins

docs.title: ✅ #218: refactor: complete AudioRecoveryRuntime migration — eliminate 24× type: ignore[attr-defined]
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:architecture

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: The P0 decomposition (#207) created AudioRecoveryRuntime (lines 45–137) with a proper _host: AudioRuntimeHost pattern. However, the old _AudioRecoveryMixin code was copied into AudioRecoveryRuntime but still contains 24× type: ignore[attr-defined] — the exact pattern that P0 was supposed to eliminate.

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py lines 147–231 in _AudioRecoveryMixin (and mirrored in AudioRecoveryRuntime._capture_audio_snapshot / _restore_audio) access host attributes via self._audio_stream, self._pcm_rx_user_callback, etc. with # type: ignore[attr-defined] on every line.

24 occurrences: 

$ grep -c "type: ignore\[attr-defined\]" src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py
24

All 24 are in _AudioRecoveryMixin. But AudioRecoveryRuntime should be using self._host._audio_stream etc. — accessing through the explicit AudioRuntimeHost Protocol.

docs.title: Expected Design
docs.text:
Following the pattern established by CivRuntime and ControlPhaseRuntime:

class AudioRecoveryRuntime:
    def __init__(self, host: AudioRuntimeHost) -> None:
        self._host = host

    def _capture_audio_snapshot(self) -> _AudioSnapshot | None:
        h = self._host
        if h._audio_stream is None:  # ← typed via Protocol, no ignore needed
            return None
        state = h._audio_stream.state
        ...

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Verify AudioRecoveryRuntime accesses all host attributes via self._host (not self)
  • [ ] Remove all type: ignore[attr-defined] from _audio_recovery.py
  • [ ] Verify AudioRuntimeHost Protocol in _runtime_protocols.py covers all accessed attributes
  • [ ] Add any missing attributes/methods to AudioRuntimeHost
  • [ ] Verify all tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration
  • [ ] Verify mypy errors in _audio_recovery.py drop to 0

Depends on #217 (dead mixin removal — remove _AudioRecoveryMixin first, then fix AudioRecoveryRuntime).

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/_audio_recovery.py — fix AudioRecoveryRuntime to use self._host
  • src/icom_lan/_runtime_protocols.py — extend AudioRuntimeHost if needed

Part of #215. Follow-up to #207 P0.

docs.title: ✅ #219: refactor: fix 197 mypy errors — capability protocol typing incomplete
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:architecture

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Issue #207 P0 stated: "mypy runs cleanly in strict mode." Current state: 197 mypy errors.

$ uv run mypy src/icom_lan/ --ignore-missing-imports --no-error-summary 2>&1 | grep "error:" | wc -l
197

docs.title: By type:
docs.text: Error type Count attr-defined 67 arg-type 41 no-untyped-call 29 union-attr 26 assignment 15 unused-ignore 7 no-any-return 4 call-overload 3 Other 5

docs.title: By file:
docs.text: File Errors Primary issue radio.py 99 Methods moved to capability protocols but still called via Radio cli.py 41 CLI calls set_attenuator, send_cw_text etc. via Radio instead of capability protocols radio_poller.py 17 no-untyped-call, arg-type audio_bridge.py 12 no-untyped-call (sounddevice) scope_render.py 11 no-untyped-call (PIL) serial.py 7 Backend typing handlers.py 5 Web handler typing Other 5 Misc

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
The Radio Protocol was correctly slimmed down (P1 task). Methods like set_attenuator, get_system_date, set_dual_watch, send_cw_text, capture_scope_frame etc. were moved to capability protocols (AdvancedControlCapable, LevelsCapable, etc.).

However, callers were not updated to narrow the type via isinstance checks before calling these methods. Example in cli.py:

# Current (broken):
await radio.set_attenuator(True)  # mypy: "Radio" has no attribute "set_attenuator"

# Correct:
if isinstance(radio, AdvancedControlCapable):
    await radio.set_attenuator(True)

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Fix cli.py — add isinstance checks for capability protocols before calling extended methods (41 errors)
  • [ ] Fix radio.py — internal calls should use self (which is Icom7610CoreRadio, not Radio), verify typing (99 errors)
  • [ ] Fix radio_poller.py — add proper type annotations (17 errors)
  • [ ] Fix audio_bridge.py — add type stubs or TYPE_CHECKING imports for sounddevice (12 errors)
  • [ ] Fix scope_render.py — add type stubs or TYPE_CHECKING imports for PIL (11 errors)
  • [ ] Fix remaining files (17 errors)
  • [ ] Remove unused-ignore suppressions (7 errors)
  • [ ] Final: uv run mypy src/icom_lan/ --ignore-missing-imports reports 0 errors
  • [ ] All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration

docs.text:

  • [ ] No new type: ignore suppressions added (net reduction)
  • [ ] All 2871 unit tests pass

docs.title: ✅ #220: refactor: add all to init.py — narrow public API surface
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P1, area:api

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Issue #207 P1 required: "Clearly define the supported public API and mark the rest as internal."

The documentation side was done (docs/api/public-api-surface.md exists and is thorough). But src/icom_lan/__init__.py still exports everything (292 lines) without __all__, so users can from icom_lan import build_civ_frame, parse_header, IcomCommander — all internal details.

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
$ wc -l src/icom_lan/__init__.py
292

No __all__ is defined. All imports are unconditional. Internal symbols like build_civ_frame, parse_header, serialize_header, IcomCommander, individual get_*/set_* command builders are freely importable.

docs.title: Expected
docs.text:
Per docs/api/public-api-surface.md, the supported public API is:

Core: - create_radio, Radio, IcomRadio - LanBackendConfig, SerialBackendConfig, BackendConfig - RadioState, RadioProfile, RADIOS, RadioModel

Capability protocols: - AudioCapable, ScopeCapable, DualReceiverCapable - LevelsCapable, MetersCapable, PowerControlCapable, StateNotifyCapable

Exceptions: - IcomLanError, ConnectionError, AuthenticationError, CommandError, TimeoutError - AudioError, AudioCodecBackendError, AudioFormatError, AudioTranscodeError

Types: - AudioRecoveryState, RadioConnectionState, Mode

Everything else should be importable from submodules for advanced use, but not advertised in __all__.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Define __all__ in src/icom_lan/__init__.py with only the supported public API symbols
  • [ ] Keep all current imports (don't break backward compat), but __all__ controls what from icom_lan import * exports
  • [ ] Verify docs/api/public-api-surface.md matches __all__
  • [ ] All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/__init__.py — add __all__

Part of #215. Follow-up to #207 P1.

docs.title: ✅ #221: refactor: expand shared state polling beyond TTL helper — deduplicate web/rigctld polling
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2, area:architecture

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Issue #207 P0 required: "Introduce a shared polling/state runtime layer used by both web and rigctld."

Current implementation (_shared_state_runtime.py) is only 48 lines — a DEFAULT_STATE_CACHE_TTL constant and an is_cache_fresh() helper. The actual polling logic remains duplicated:

  • src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py — web-specific polling (248 lines)
  • src/icom_lan/rigctld/poller.py — rigctld-specific polling

Both implement their own poll-interval logic, error handling, and cache write patterns.

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
# _shared_state_runtime.py (entire file):
DEFAULT_STATE_CACHE_TTL: Final[float] = 0.2

def is_cache_fresh(cache, field, max_age_s) -> bool:
    if max_age_s is None or max_age_s <= 0.0:
        return False
    return cache.is_fresh(field, max_age_s)

This is better than nothing (shared TTL prevents drift), but falls short of "shared polling/state runtime layer."

docs.title: Option A: SharedStatePoller (full unification)
docs.text:
Extract common polling logic into a shared class:

class SharedStatePoller:
    """Polls radio state (freq, mode, etc.) on a configurable interval."""

    def __init__(self, radio: Radio, cache: StateCache, interval: float = 0.2):
        ...

    async def poll_once(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Poll all standard fields, update cache, return snapshot."""
        ...

    async def run(self) -> None:
        """Background polling loop."""
        ...

Web and rigctld would both use SharedStatePoller and add their own projection/delivery on top.

docs.title: Option B: Shared poll helpers (lighter touch)
docs.text:
Keep separate pollers but extract shared "what to poll and how" into _shared_state_runtime.py:

async def poll_frequency(radio: Radio, cache: StateCache) -> int | None: ...
async def poll_mode(radio: Radio, cache: StateCache) -> tuple[Mode, int] | None: ...
async def poll_standard_fields(radio: Radio, cache: StateCache) -> dict: ...

docs.title: Recommendation
docs.text: Option B is more pragmatic — web and rigctld have different polling intervals, different field sets, and different error handling needs. Full unification (Option A) may over-constrain them.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Analyze differences between web/radio_poller.py and rigctld/poller.py polling logic
  • [ ] Extract shared "poll field X with cache check" helpers into _shared_state_runtime.py
  • [ ] Update both pollers to use shared helpers
  • [ ] Verify behavior parity: web and rigctld return same values for same radio state
  • [ ] All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• src/icom_lan/_shared_state_runtime.py — expand with shared poll helpers
  • src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py — refactor to use shared helpers
  • src/icom_lan/rigctld/poller.py — refactor to use shared helpers

Part of #215. Follow-up to #207 P0.

docs.title: ✅ #222: epic: Serial port auto-discovery for Icom radios
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:serial

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Automatic discovery of Icom radios on USB serial ports. Extend icom-lan discover to scan serial devices, auto-detect baud rate, probe CI-V addresses, and identify radio models — zero manual configuration needed.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text:
Current state: User must manually specify serial port path and baud rate: 

export ICOM_SERIAL_PORT=/dev/cu.usbserial-11320
export ICOM_SERIAL_BAUD=19200
icom-lan status

Problem: Requires trial-and-error (wrong port → timeout, wrong baud → garbage).

Goal: Plug-and-play experience matching LAN discovery: 

icom-lan discover
# Output:
# LAN radios:
#   IC-7610 @ 192.168.55.40 (LAN)
#
# Serial radios:
#   IC-7610 (0x98) @ /dev/cu.usbserial-11320 (19200 baud)
#   IC-705 (0xA4) @ /dev/cu.usbserial-54321 (115200 baud)

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[icom-lan discover] --> B[LAN Discovery]
    A --> C[Serial Discovery]
    C --> D[list_ports.comports]
    D --> E[Filter candidates]
    E --> F[Probe each port]
    F --> G{CI-V response?}
    G -->|Yes| H[Parse model + address]
    G -->|No| I[Skip]
    H --> J[Return radio info]

docs.title: 1. Serial Port Enumeration
docs.text:
• Use pyserial.tools.list_ports.comports()
  • Filter candidates:
  • USB serial devices (exclude Bluetooth, virtual)
  • Known Icom VID (if available in metadata)
  • Skip audio-only devices (macOS IC-7610 creates 2 ports: CI-V + audio)

docs.title: 2. Baud Rate Auto-Detection
docs.text:
• Try common rates in order: [19200, 9600, 115200, 4800]
  • Stop on first successful CI-V response
  • Default: 19200 (most common for Icom)

docs.title: 3. CI-V Probing
docs.text:
• Send Read Transceiver ID command: FE FE 00 E0 19 00 FD
  • 00 = broadcast address (any radio responds)
  • 19 00 = "who are you?"
  • Parse response: FE FE E0 <addr> 19 00 <model_id> FD
  • Extract <addr> (e.g. 0x98 for IC-7610)
  • Extract <model_id> (2 bytes BCD, e.g. 0x01 0x06 = IC-7610)

docs.title: 4. Model Identification
docs.text:
• Map CI-V address + model ID → radio name
  • Table: 
    RADIO_MAP = {
    0x98: ("IC-7610", b"\x01\x06"),
    0xA4: ("IC-705", b"\x01\x05"),
    0x94: ("IC-7300", b"\x01\x01"),
    0xA2: ("IC-9700", b"\x01\x20"),
    # ...
}

docs.title: 5. Integration with icom-lan discover
docs.text:
• Unified output: LAN + serial results in one table
  • Priority: LAN first (faster), then serial (slower)
  • Parallel: Probe serial ports concurrently (asyncio)

docs.title: ✅ #223: feat: Serial port enumeration with filtering (Epic #222 Phase 1)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:serial

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Implement serial port enumeration with candidate filtering for Icom radio discovery.

Part of Epic #222 — Phase 1.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Implement enumerate_serial_ports() in new src/icom_lan/discovery.py
  • [ ] Filter logic:
  • Include: USB serial devices
  • Exclude: Bluetooth (/dev/cu.Bluetooth-*)
  • Exclude: virtual ports (/dev/cu.debug-*, /dev/cu.wlan-*)
  • Exclude: audio-only devices (macOS IC-7610 creates 2 ports — skip audio)
  • [ ] Return list of SerialPortCandidate (path, description, hwid)
  • [ ] Unit tests with mocked list_ports.comports()

docs.title: Design
docs.text:
@dataclass
class SerialPortCandidate:
    device: str           # /dev/cu.usbserial-11320
    description: str      # "USB Serial"
    hwid: str | None      # Hardware ID

def enumerate_serial_ports() -> list[SerialPortCandidate]:
    """Enumerate candidate USB serial ports for Icom radios."""
    from serial.tools import list_ports

    candidates = []
    for port in list_ports.comports():
        if _is_candidate(port):
            candidates.append(SerialPortCandidate(
                device=port.device,
                description=port.description,
                hwid=port.hwid,
            ))
    return candidates

def _is_candidate(port) -> bool:
    """Filter out non-USB, Bluetooth, virtual ports."""
    # Exclude Bluetooth
    if "Bluetooth" in port.device:
        return False
    # Exclude virtual
    if any(x in port.device for x in ["debug", "wlan", "spi"]):
        return False
    # Include USB serial
    if "usb" in port.device.lower():
        return True
    return False

docs.title: ✅ #224: feat: CI-V probing with auto baud detection (Epic #222 Phase 2)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:serial

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Implement CI-V probing over serial with auto baud-rate detection.

Part of Epic #222 — Phase 2.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Implement probe_serial_civ(port: str, baud_rates: list[int]) → CivProbeResult | None
  • [ ] CI-V frame construction: FE FE 00 E0 19 00 FD (Read Transceiver ID, broadcast)
  • [ ] Response parsing: FE FE E0 <addr> 19 00 <model_id> FD
  • [ ] Baud rate iteration: [19200, 9600, 115200, 4800]
  • [ ] Timeout: 1s per baud rate attempt
  • [ ] Unit tests with mock serial transport

docs.title: Design
docs.text:
@dataclass
class CivProbeResult:
    port: str
    baud: int
    address: int       # CI-V address (0x98, 0xA4, etc.)
    model_id: bytes    # 2 bytes BCD

async def probe_serial_civ(
    port: str,
    baud_rates: list[int] = [19200, 9600, 115200, 4800],
    timeout: float = 1.0,
) -> CivProbeResult | None:
    """Probe serial port for CI-V radio."""
    for baud in baud_rates:
        try:
            result = await _try_baud(port, baud, timeout)
            if result:
                return result
        except SerialException:
            continue
    return None

async def _try_baud(port: str, baud: int, timeout: float) -> CivProbeResult | None:
    """Open serial, send CI-V probe, parse response."""
    # Open serial port
    reader, writer = await serial_asyncio.open_serial_connection(
        url=port, baudrate=baud, timeout=timeout
    )

    # Send: FE FE 00 E0 19 00 FD (Read Transceiver ID, broadcast)
    cmd = bytes([0xFE, 0xFE, 0x00, 0xE0, 0x19, 0x00, 0xFD])
    writer.write(cmd)
    await writer.drain()

    # Read response (max 64 bytes, timeout 1s)
    try:
        data = await asyncio.wait_for(reader.read(64), timeout=timeout)
    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        writer.close()
        await writer.wait_closed()
        return None

    # Parse: FE FE E0 <addr> 19 00 <model_id> FD
    if len(data) < 9 or data[0:2] != b'\xFE\xFE':
        writer.close()
        await writer.wait_closed()
        return None

    address = data[3]
    model_id = data[6:8]  # 2 bytes BCD

    writer.close()
    await writer.wait_closed()

    return CivProbeResult(
        port=port,
        baud=baud,
        address=address,
        model_id=model_id,
    )

docs.title: ✅ #225: feat: Radio model identification from CI-V data (Epic #222 Phase 3)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:serial

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Radio model identification from CI-V address and model ID bytes.

Part of Epic #222 — Phase 3.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Add SERIAL_RADIO_MAP to src/icom_lan/radios.py
  • [ ] Implement identify_radio(address: int, model_id: bytes) -> str | None
  • [ ] Support IC-7610, IC-705, IC-7300, IC-9700 (MVP)
  • [ ] Fallback: return f"Unknown (0x{address:02X})" if not in map
  • [ ] Unit tests for all supported models

docs.title: Design
docs.text:
# src/icom_lan/radios.py

SERIAL_RADIO_MAP: dict[int, tuple[str, bytes]] = {
    # address: (name, model_id_bcd)
    0x98: ("IC-7610", b"\x01\x06"),
    0xA4: ("IC-705", b"\x01\x05"),
    0x94: ("IC-7300", b"\x01\x01"),
    0xA2: ("IC-9700", b"\x01\x20"),
    0x8E: ("IC-7851", b"\x01\x35"),
    0x96: ("IC-R8600", b"\x01\x26"),
}

def identify_radio(address: int, model_id: bytes) -> str:
    """Identify radio from CI-V address and model ID.

    Args:
        address: CI-V address (e.g. 0x98 for IC-7610)
        model_id: 2-byte BCD model ID from 0x19 0x00 response

    Returns:
        Radio name (e.g. "IC-7610") or "Unknown (0xXX)"
    """
    entry = SERIAL_RADIO_MAP.get(address)
    if not entry:
        return f"Unknown (0x{address:02X})"

    expected_name, expected_id = entry

    # Verify model ID matches (optional strict check)
    if model_id != expected_id:
        logger.warning(
            f"CI-V address {address:02X} maps to {expected_name}, "
            f"but model ID {model_id.hex()} != {expected_id.hex()}"
        )

    return expected_name

docs.title: CLI Command Update
docs.text:
# src/icom_lan/cli.py

@app.command()
def discover(
    serial_only: bool = typer.Option(False, "--serial-only", help="Only scan serial ports"),
    lan_only: bool = typer.Option(False, "--lan-only", help="Only scan LAN"),
    timeout: float = typer.Option(5.0, "--timeout", help="Discovery timeout (seconds)"),
):
    """Discover Icom radios on LAN and serial ports."""
    import asyncio
    from icom_lan.discovery import discover_lan_radios, discover_serial_radios

    async def _run():
        tasks = []
        if not serial_only:
            tasks.append(discover_lan_radios(timeout=timeout))
        if not lan_only:
            tasks.append(discover_serial_radios())

        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

        lan_radios = results[0] if not serial_only else []
        serial_radios = results[1] if not lan_only else []

        # Print results
        if lan_radios:
            typer.echo("LAN radios:")
            for radio in lan_radios:
                typer.echo(f"  {radio.model} @ {radio.host} (LAN)")

        if serial_radios:
            typer.echo("\nSerial radios:")
            for radio in serial_radios:
                typer.echo(f"  {radio.model} (0x{radio.address:02X}) @ {radio.port} ({radio.baud} baud)")

        if not lan_radios and not serial_radios:
            typer.echo("No radios found.")

    asyncio.run(_run())

docs.title: ⬜ #227: lint: Clean up 94 unused imports in init.py (F401)
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Fix 94 ruff F401 (unused-import) warnings in src/icom_lan/__init__.py.

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
__init__.py imports 94 symbols that are not used in the file itself, causing ruff to flag them as unused:

F401 .auth.AuthResponse imported but unused; consider removing, adding to __all__, or using a redundant alias
F401 .auth.StatusResponse imported but unused; ...
... (94 total)

Root cause: These symbols are imported for public API exposure but are not in __all__ (which currently has 44 symbols).

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
uv run ruff check src/icom_lan/__init__.py --select F401

Expected: 94 F401 errors.

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
Option A (recommended): Add all 94 symbols to __all__ if they are part of public API:

__all__ = [
    # Existing 44 symbols
    "IcomRadio",
    "create_radio",
    # ...

    # Add these 94:
    "AuthResponse",
    "StatusResponse",
    "build_conninfo_packet",
    # ... etc
]

Option B: Remove imports that are not part of public API (internal helpers only).

Option C: Add redundant aliases (AuthResponse as AuthResponse) — but this is noisy.

docs.text:

  • [ ] Public API surface unchanged (or explicitly documented if reduced)
  • [ ] __all__ accurately reflects exported symbols
  • [ ] All tests pass

docs.title: ⬜ #228: lint: Fix 6 module import order warnings (E402)
docs.text: Labels: type:tech-debt, priority:P2

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Fix 6 ruff E402 (module-import-not-at-top-of-file) warnings in sync.py and _shared_state_runtime.py.

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Imports are placed after module-level code (TypeVar definitions, docstrings), causing ruff to flag them:

E402 Module level import not at top of file
  --> src/icom_lan/_shared_state_runtime.py:19:1
E402 Module level import not at top of file
  --> src/icom_lan/sync.py:21:1
... (6 total)

Affected files: - src/icom_lan/_shared_state_runtime.py (2 warnings) — imports after docstring - src/icom_lan/sync.py (4 warnings) — imports after TypeVar("T") definition

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: Intentional pattern to avoid: 1. Circular import issues (sync.py needs TypeVar defined before importing classes that use it) 2. PEP 257 compliance (docstring before imports in _shared_state_runtime.py)

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
uv run ruff check src/ --select E402

Expected: 6 E402 errors.

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
Option A (quick fix): Add # noqa: E402 to suppress warnings:

# src/icom_lan/sync.py
T = TypeVar("T")

from .audio import AudioPacket  # noqa: E402
from .radio import IcomRadio as _AsyncIcomRadio  # noqa: E402
# ... etc

Option B (refactor): Restructure to move TypeVar/docstring logic: - Move TypeVar to separate _types.py module - Move docstring to end of file (non-standard)

Recommendation: Option A — cleanest, preserves working circular-import avoidance.

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:

  • [ ] No circular import regressions
  • [ ] All tests pass

docs.title: ⬜ #229: Fix flaky test: test_soft_reconnect_handles_connect_failure
docs.text: Labels: bug

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
test_soft_reconnect_handles_connect_failure is currently skipped in CI due to mock not preventing real IcomTransport.connect() execution.

Symptom: Test hangs in CI with 10 retry attempts (~50 seconds), then fails with TimeoutError. Status: Passes locally, fails in CI.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: Mock patch on icom_lan.radio.IcomTransport doesn't intercept transport creation inside soft_reconnect() in CI environment. Possible async timing or import caching issue.

docs.title: Current Workaround
docs.text: Test is skipped with @pytest.mark.skip to achieve green CI.

docs.title: Fix Options
docs.text:
1. Patch at a different level (e.g., patch IcomTransport.__init__)
  2. Use fixture-level mock instead of inline patch
  3. Refactor soft_reconnect() to make transport creation injectable
  4. Accept as integration test and run only locally

docs.title: ⬜ #230: Fix flaky test: test_audio_bridge_smoke_with_serial_backend_audio_driver
docs.text: Labels: bug

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
test_audio_bridge_smoke_with_serial_backend_audio_driver is currently skipped in CI due to race condition in audio frame propagation timing.

Symptom: Test assertion assert fake_sd.output_stream.writes fails (empty list) in CI. Status: Passes locally, fails in CI.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: Audio frames don't propagate through the bridge within the 1-second deadline in CI environment. Likely due to: - Slower async event loop scheduling in CI - Different timing characteristics of CI runners - Potential sounddevice mock setup delay

docs.title: Current Workaround
docs.text: Test is skipped with @pytest.mark.skip to achieve green CI.

docs.title: Fix Options
docs.text:
1. Increase deadline from 1.0s to 5.0s
  2. Add explicit frame flush/pump mechanism
  3. Use more reliable synchronization (e.g., asyncio.Event instead of polling)
  4. Mock at a higher level to avoid timing dependency
  5. Accept as integration test requiring real audio device

docs.title: Problem Backend sends {"status":"error"} but frontend ignores → no user feedback on failures. ## Location ws-client.ts message handler ## Fix Add response handling, show toast/console error on failures. ## Epic Part of #169 (Critical Bugs)

docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem onChange listener registered but never cleaned up → memory leak on component unmount. ## Location BottomBar.svelte ## Fix Use $effect cleanup or explicit onDestroy. ## Epic Part of #169 (Critical Bugs)
✅ #236: arch: Replace triple HTTP polling with WS state push
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem 3 separate XHR polls (/state, /capabilities, /bridge) every 200-500ms → inefficient, high latency. ## Solution Push state updates via control WebSocket. ## Dependencies Blocks #176 (reduce WS conns), enables #1710 (state delta). ## Epic Part of #169 (Architecture)
✅ #237: arch: Reduce WebSocket connections (iOS 6-conn limit)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title:
Problem
iOS Safari has 6 HTTP connection limit. 4 WS (control/scope/meters/audio) + keepalive = problems.

## Solution
Merge control/meters into one WS, or use HTTP/2.

## Dependencies
Requires #175 (WS state push).

## Epic
Part of #169 (Architecture)
✅ #238: arch: Remove Connection:close, enable HTTP keepalive
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title: Problem Backend forces Connection: close → every request = new TCP handshake. ## Solution Remove forced close, let browser reuse connections. ## Epic Part of #169 (Architecture)
✅ #239: bug: Optimistic update frequency snap mismatch
docs.text: Labels: bug, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title:
Problem
Optimistic freq updates snap to different precision than server → visual flicker.

## Solution
Match client-side rounding to server logic.

## Epic
Part of #169 (Architecture)
✅ #240: resilience: XHR polling error backoff + stale indicator
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title:
Problem
XHR poll failures retry immediately → hammers server, no user feedback.

## Solution
Exponential backoff (200ms → 5s), show "reconnecting" indicator.

## Dependencies
Enables #1703 (stale state detection).

## Epic
Part of #169 (Resilience)
✅ #241: resilience: PTT button timeout protection
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P0, area:web-ui

docs.title:
Problem
PTT stuck = transmit forever if WS drops.

## Solution
Client-side timeout (30s max), auto-release on WS disconnect.

## Epic
Part of #169 (Resilience)
✅ #242: resilience: Stale state detection and warning
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1, area:web-ui

docs.title:
Problem
No indication when state is outdated (poll stopped, WS dead).

## Solution
Timestamp checks, show "stale data" warning after 5s.

## Epic
Part of #169 (Resilience)
✅ #243: resilience: Coordinate Audio/Control WS state
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title:
Problem
Audio WS can be alive while control WS is dead → confusing state.

## Solution
Unified connection health store, disable audio UI when control down.

## Dependencies
Requires #175 (WS state push).

## Epic
Part of #169 (Resilience)
✅ #244: resilience: App.svelte limit reload retries
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.title:
Problem
Infinite reload loop on persistent errors.

## Solution
Max 3 retries, then show error message.

docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P2, area:web-ui

Waterfall/spectrum render when tab inactive → wasted CPU.

## Solution
Use Page Visibility API to pause RAF loop.

docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P3, area:web-ui

docs.title:

No auth → anyone on LAN can control radio.

## Solution
Optional token-based auth, configured via env var.

docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P3, area:web-ui

docs.title: ✅ #252: feat: Rig TOML schema design + format spec (#251 Phase 0)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Design the TOML schema for rig configuration files and document it in rigs/_schema.md.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
1. rigs/_schema.md — human-readable format specification
  2. Example snippet showing all sections with comments
  3. Validation rules (required vs optional fields, value constraints)

docs.title: Schema Sections
docs.text:
[radio]           # Model identity, CIV address, receiver count
[spectrum]        # Scope parameters (seq_max, amp_max, len_max)
[capabilities]    # Feature flags (list of capability strings)
[freq_ranges]     # Supported frequency ranges with band plans
[modes]           # Supported operating modes
[filters]         # Available IF filters
[vfo]             # VFO scheme (ab vs main_sub) + wire bytes
[commands]        # Default CI-V command wire bytes
[commands.overrides]  # Model-specific wire byte overrides

docs.title: Design Constraints
docs.text:
• Use Python stdlib tomllib (3.11+) — no external deps
  • All wire bytes as hex integer arrays: [0x14, 0x01]
  • Capabilities as string lists matching existing RadioProfile.capabilities
  • Must support inheritance/defaults: commands shared across models defined once
  • Schema must be self-documenting (comments in TOML)

docs.title: Agent Instructions
docs.text:
1. Read current src/icom_lan/profiles.py for existing RadioProfile fields
  2. Read references/wfview/rigs/IC-7610.rig and IC-7300.rig for reference data
  3. Read src/icom_lan/commands.py for all command wire bytes
  4. Design schema that captures ALL data currently in profiles.py + commands.py
  5. Write rigs/_schema.md with full specification
  6. Create example rigs/_template.toml with all fields commented

docs.title: TDD
docs.text:
• Write tests/test_rig_schema.py with schema validation tests FIRST
  • Test: valid TOML parses without errors
  • Test: missing required fields raise clear errors
  • Test: invalid capability names rejected
  • Test: wire byte arrays validated (non-empty, valid hex range 0x00-0xFF)

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] rigs/_schema.md documents every field with type, required/optional, description
  • [ ] rigs/_template.toml is valid TOML and parseable
  • [ ] Schema validation tests pass
  • [ ] Schema covers 100% of RadioProfile fields
  • [ ] Schema covers 100% of command wire bytes in commands.py

docs.title: ✅ #253: feat: rig_loader.py — TOML parser + validator + RadioProfile builder (#251 Phase 1)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Implement src/icom_lan/rig_loader.py — the runtime engine that loads .toml rig files and produces RadioProfile + CommandMap objects.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
1. src/icom_lan/rig_loader.py with:
  2. load_rig(path: Path) -> RigConfig — parse + validate single TOML file
  3. discover_rigs(rigs_dir: Path) -> dict[str, RigConfig] — load all rigs from directory
  4. RigConfig dataclass holding parsed data
  5. build_profile(rig: RigConfig) -> RadioProfile — convert to existing RadioProfile
  6. build_command_map(rig: RigConfig) -> CommandMap — build wire byte lookup

  7. Validation with clear error messages (missing fields, invalid bytes, etc.)

  8. src/icom_lan/command_map.py with:

  9. CommandMap class — get(command_name: str) -> tuple[int, ...]
  10. Default commands (shared across all Icom radios) + per-model overrides
  11. __contains__ for capability checks

docs.title: Design
docs.text:
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CommandMap:
    """Rig-specific CI-V command wire byte mapping."""
    _commands: dict[str, tuple[int, ...]]

    def get(self, name: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
        """Get wire bytes for command name. Raises KeyError if unsupported."""
        ...

    def has(self, name: str) -> bool:
        """Check if command is supported by this rig."""
        ...

docs.title: TDD — Write Tests FIRST
docs.text:
tests/test_rig_loader.py:

# Test: load valid TOML → RigConfig with all fields populated
# Test: load TOML with missing required field → RigLoadError with field name
# Test: load TOML with invalid CIV address (>0xFF) → validation error
# Test: load TOML with empty wire bytes → validation error
# Test: build_profile produces RadioProfile matching current IC-7610 hardcoded profile
# Test: build_command_map produces CommandMap with all expected commands
# Test: CommandMap.get returns correct wire bytes
# Test: CommandMap.has returns False for unsupported commands
# Test: discover_rigs loads all .toml files from directory
# Test: discover_rigs ignores files starting with underscore (_schema.md, _template.toml)

docs.title: Agent Instructions
docs.text:
1. Read rigs/_schema.md (from #252) for field definitions
  2. Read src/icom_lan/profiles.py for RadioProfile dataclass

  4. Implement src/icom_lan/rig_loader.py to pass tests
  5. Implement src/icom_lan/command_map.py to pass tests
  6. Run full test suite — zero regressions

• Python stdlib only (tomllib, dataclasses, pathlib)
  • Frozen dataclasses (immutable after load)

  • Clear error messages: RigLoadError("ic7300.toml: missing required field [radio].civ_address")

docs.title: ✅ #254: feat: ic7610.toml — migrate IC-7610 profile from Python to TOML (#251 Phase 2)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

docs.title: Goal
docs.text:
Create rigs/ic7610.toml — the reference rig file for IC-7610, migrating all hardcoded data from profiles.py and commands.py into TOML format.

This is the most critical sub-issue: IC-7610 is our primary radio and the file must be byte-for-byte accurate.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
1. rigs/ic7610.toml — complete IC-7610 rig configuration
  2. Verification test that loaded TOML matches current hardcoded data exactly

docs.title: Data Sources (authoritative)
docs.text:
1. src/icom_lan/profiles.py → _PROFILES["IC-7610"] (capabilities, civ_addr, cmd29_routes, vfo codes)
  2. src/icom_lan/commands.py → all build_frame() calls (wire bytes for every command)
  3. references/wfview/rigs/IC-7610.rig → cross-reference (134 commands, spectrum params)
  4. docs/parity/ic7610_command_matrix.json → parity status for all 134 commands

docs.title: Key IC-7610 Properties
docs.text:
CIV address: 0x98
Receivers: 2 (Main + Sub)
Command29: true (selective, see cmd29_routes)
Spectrum: true (via LAN UDP, not CI-V)
LAN: true
VFO scheme: main_sub (0xD0=Main, 0xD1=Sub, 0xB0=Swap)

# Test: loaded profile.capabilities == current _DUAL_CAPS
# Test: loaded cmd29_routes match current _CMD29_7610 exactly
# Test: loaded vfo_main_code == 0xD0, vfo_sub_code == 0xD1, vfo_swap_code == 0xB0
# Test: CommandMap has ALL commands from commands.py (complete parity)
# Test: CommandMap wire bytes match hardcoded values for EVERY command
# Test: spectrum params match wfview reference (seq_max, amp_max, len_max)
# Test: freq_ranges cover 30kHz-60MHz
# Test: modes include all 8 HF modes

  7. Run full suite — zero regressions

✅ #255: refactor: Wire commands.py to use CommandMap from rig profiles (#251 Phase 3)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, type:tech-debt, priority:P1

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Refactor src/icom_lan/commands.py so command builders use CommandMap wire bytes from loaded rig profiles instead of hardcoded byte literals. This is the core refactor that makes multi-radio support work.

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
# commands.py — hardcoded IC-7610 wire bytes
def get_af_gain() -> bytes:
    return bytes([0xFE, 0xFE, ADDR, 0xE0, 0x14, 0x01, 0xFD])

def get_s_meter() -> bytes:
    return bytes([0xFE, 0xFE, ADDR, 0xE0, 0x15, 0x02, 0xFD])

docs.title: Target State
docs.text:
# commands.py — rig-driven wire bytes
def get_af_gain(cmd_map: CommandMap) -> bytes:
    return build_frame(cmd_map.get("af_gain"))

def get_s_meter(cmd_map: CommandMap) -> bytes:
    return build_frame(cmd_map.get("s_meter"))

docs.title: Strategy: Incremental Migration
docs.text: Do NOT rewrite commands.py in one pass. Use adapter pattern:

docs.title: Step 1: Add CommandMap parameter with backward-compatible default
docs.text:
_DEFAULT_CMD_MAP: CommandMap | None = None  # Set at startup from rig loader

def get_af_gain(cmd_map: CommandMap | None = None) -> bytes:
    cm = cmd_map or _DEFAULT_CMD_MAP
    if cm is not None:
        return build_frame(cm.get("af_gain"))
    # Legacy fallback: hardcoded bytes
    return bytes([0xFE, 0xFE, ADDR, 0xE0, 0x14, 0x01, 0xFD])

docs.title: Step 2: Wire rig loader into startup (set _DEFAULT_CMD_MAP)
Step 3: Verify all tests still pass with rig-loaded CommandMap
Step 4: Remove legacy hardcoded fallbacks (once confident)
TDD — Write Tests FIRST
docs.text:
tests/test_command_map_integration.py:

# Test: every command in commands.py has matching entry in ic7610 CommandMap
# Test: build_frame with CommandMap produces identical bytes as hardcoded version
# Test: CommandMap override (ic7300) produces different bytes for overridden commands
# Test: CommandMap override does NOT affect non-overridden commands
# Test: missing command in CommandMap raises clear error (not silent fallback)
# Test: all callers of commands.py (radio.py, handlers.py, poller) work with CommandMap

docs.title: Agent Instructions
docs.text:
1. Read src/icom_lan/commands.py COMPLETELY — understand every function
  2. Read src/icom_lan/command_map.py (from #253) — understand CommandMap API
  3. Count all command functions: grep -c "^def " src/icom_lan/commands.py
  4. Write tests FIRST
  5. Implement Step 1 (add CommandMap param with default)
  6. Run full suite after EACH step — zero regressions at every checkpoint
  7. Do NOT remove legacy fallbacks yet (Step 4 is separate)

docs.title: Risk Mitigation
docs.text:
• Highest risk refactor in this epic — touches all command paths
  • Every existing test MUST pass at every intermediate step
  • Backward-compatible default means no caller changes needed initially
  • Radio.py, handlers.py, poller.py callers updated AFTER commands.py is stable

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] All command builders accept optional CommandMap parameter
  • [ ] When CommandMap provided, wire bytes come from map (not hardcoded)
  • [ ] When CommandMap is None, legacy hardcoded bytes used (backward compat)
  • [ ] ic7610.toml CommandMap produces identical output to hardcoded bytes
  • [ ] All 3200+ existing tests pass (zero regressions)
  • [ ] New tests: ≥10 integration tests

  • src/icom_lan/radio.py (wire CommandMap through)
  • src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py (wire CommandMap through)
  • src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py (wire CommandMap through)

  • rigs/ic7610.toml (from #254)

✅ #256: feat: ic7300.toml — IC-7300 rig file with command overrides (#251 Phase 4)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Create rigs/ic7300.toml — complete IC-7300 rig configuration with command wire byte overrides where they differ from IC-7610.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
1. rigs/ic7300.toml — IC-7300 rig configuration
  2. Tests verifying overrides and unique capabilities

docs.title: IC-7300 Key Properties
docs.text:
CIV address: 0x94
Receivers: 1 (single)
Command29: false (none)
Spectrum: true (via CI-V serial, SpectrumSeqMax=11, LenMax=475)
LAN: false
VFO scheme: ab (A=0x07 0x00, B=0x07 0x01, Equal=0x07 0xA0, Swap=0x07 0xB0)

docs.title: Command Wire Overrides (14 commands differ from IC-7610)
docs.text:
From wfview reference analysis:

Command IC-7610 IC-7300 acc1_mod_level 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x88 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x64 usb_mod_level 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x89 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x65 data_off_mod_input 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x91 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x66 data1_mod_input 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x92 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x67 civ_transceive 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x12 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x71 system_date 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x58 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x94 system_time 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x59 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x95 utc_offset 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x62 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x96 quick_split 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x33 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x30 nb_depth 0x1A 0x05 0x02 0x90 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x89 nb_width 0x1A 0x05 0x02 0x91 0x1A 0x05 0x01 0x90 s_meter_sql_status 0x15 0x01 0x16 0x43 civ_output_ant 0x1C 0x04 0x1A 0x05 0x00 0x61 agc_time_constant 0x1A 0x04 0x1A 0x04

docs.title: Capabilities (IC-7300 _SINGLE_CAPS + extras)
docs.text:
Core: audio, scope, meters, tx, cw, rf_gain, af_level, squelch DSP: attenuator, preamp, nb, nr Unique: audio_eq (SSB/AM/FM Bass/Treble controls)

NOT supported: dual_rx, digisel, ip_plus, drive_gain, rx_antenna

docs.title: Commands NOT available on IC-7300
docs.text: These commands from IC-7610 should NOT be in ic7300.toml: - Main/Sub Prefix (0x29), VFO Dual Watch, VFO Main/Sub Select - RX Antenna, DIGI-SEL Shift, Drive Gain, Data Mode Filter - Quick Dual Watch, LAN Mod Level, DATA2/DATA3 Mod Input - XFC Status, Scope Main/Sub

docs.title: TDD — Write Tests FIRST
docs.text:
tests/test_rig_ic7300.py:

# Test: ic7300.toml loads without validation errors
# Test: profile.civ_addr == 0x94
# Test: profile.receiver_count == 1
# Test: profile.capabilities includes attenuator, preamp, nb, nr
# Test: profile.capabilities does NOT include dual_rx, digisel, ip_plus
# Test: CommandMap override: s_meter_sql_status == (0x16, 0x43)
# Test: CommandMap override: system_date == (0x1A, 0x05, 0x00, 0x94)
# Test: CommandMap non-override: get_freq == same as IC-7610
# Test: CommandMap non-override: get_s_meter == same as IC-7610
# Test: CommandMap does NOT have dual_rx commands
# Test: vfo scheme is "ab" with correct wire bytes
# Test: cmd29_routes is empty (no Command29)
# Test: spectrum params match wfview (seq_max=11, amp_max=160, len_max=475)

docs.text:
1. Read rigs/ic7610.toml (from #254) as template
  2. Read references/wfview/rigs/IC-7300.rig for all IC-7300 data
  3. Read the override table above for the 14 differing commands
  4. Write tests FIRST
  5. Create rigs/ic7300.toml modifying only what differs
  6. Run full suite — zero regressions

docs.title: ✅ #257: test: IC-7300 serial smoke test + hardware validation (#251 Phase 5)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, priority:P1

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Validate IC-7300 works end-to-end via USB serial: discovery → connect → freq/mode/PTT/meters/scope.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• IC-7300 connected via USB (CI-V serial + USB audio)
  • All previous phases merged (#252-#256)

docs.title: 1. Discovery
docs.text:
icom-lan discover --serial-only
# Expected: IC-7300 found at /dev/cu.usbserial-XXXX, baud=19200 (or auto), CIV addr=0x94

docs.title: 2. Basic Commands

icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial --serial-port /dev/cu.usbserial-XXXX status
icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial freq
icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial mode
icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial meter

docs.title: 3. Web UI
docs.text:
icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial web --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080
# Verify: Web UI shows single receiver, no SUB controls
# Verify: Frequency/mode display correct
# Verify: Meters (S-meter, power, SWR) updating
# Verify: Scope/waterfall rendering (if supported via CI-V serial)

# Test with WSJT-X or rigctl client

icom-lan --rig ic7300 --backend serial --list-audio-devices
# Verify IC-7300 USB audio device detected
# Test RX audio playback

  • Working baud rate
  • Any commands that fail (log with exact CI-V bytes)

  • Audio device names
  • Any IC-7300-specific quirks

  • [ ] rigctld serves correct frequency/mode
  • [ ] Any failures documented with exact CI-V trace

When capabilities are missing: - Hide DIGI-SEL controls if digisel not in capabilities - Hide IP+ toggle if ip_plus not in capabilities - Hide RX Antenna selector if no rx_antenna capability

docs.title: ✅ #260: feat: CLI --model flag + auto-resolve radio_addr from rig profiles
docs.text: Labels: enhancement

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: CLI and API should auto-resolve radio_addr and defaults from rig TOML profiles.

docs.title: Current Problem
docs.text:
# Works (IC-7610 default):
icom-lan --backend serial --serial-port /dev/cu.usbserial-111 status

# Fails (IC-7300 — no way to set radio_addr via CLI):
icom-lan --backend serial --serial-port /dev/cu.usbserial-201 status
# → Uses 0x98 (IC-7610) → timeout

docs.title: Desired
docs.text:
# Auto-resolve from rig profile:
icom-lan --model IC-7300 --backend serial --serial-port /dev/cu.usbserial-201 status
# → radio_addr=0x94, single receiver, VFO A/B

# Manual override (takes priority):
icom-lan --model IC-7300 --radio-addr 0xA0 --backend serial ... status

docs.title: 1. CLI arguments
docs.text:
• --model MODEL — radio model name (matches rigs/*.toml id or model name)
  • --radio-addr ADDR — explicit CI-V address override (hex or decimal)

docs.title: 2. Resolution order (for radio_addr)
docs.text:
1. --radio-addr (explicit override, highest priority)
  2. --model → lookup rigs/<model>.toml → [radio].civ_addr
  3. Environment var ICOM_RADIO_ADDR (optional)
  4. Default: 0x98 (IC-7610 backward compat)

docs.title: 3. Config wiring
docs.text:
• _build_config() in cli.py resolves model → rig profile → fills config fields
  • SerialBackendConfig and LanBackendConfig already have radio_addr and model
  • Just need to wire the CLI args through

docs.title: 4. Web UI model detection
docs.text:
• When --model is given, WebServer uses it for profile resolution
  • /api/v1/info reflects correct model and capabilities

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• [ ] --model IC-7300 resolves radio_addr=0x94 from TOML
  • [ ] --radio-addr 0x94 works standalone
  • [ ] --radio-addr overrides profile default
  • [ ] Default (no flags) = IC-7610 backward compat
  • [ ] Works for both serial and LAN backends
  • [ ] All existing tests pass
  • [ ] --model foo gives clear error with available models

docs.title: Related
docs.text: • Epic #251

docs.title: ✅ #261: bug: serial backend — scope health + read error flood on IC-7300
docs.text: Labels: bug

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: When running web UI with IC-7300 over USB serial, two issues cause log flood and high CPU:

docs.title: 1. Scope health monitor retries infinitely
docs.text: IC-7300 scope via CI-V serial uses different commands than IC-7610 LAN scope. The health monitor detects all-zero frames, re-enables scope in a loop. On IC-7300 the scope data never comes via this path → infinite retry.

docs.title: 2. Serial read error has no backoff
docs.text: Recoverable serial read error: device reports readiness to read but returned no data fires thousands of times per second with no delay → 34% CPU, 78k log lines in 5 seconds.

docs.title: Expected Behavior
docs.text:
1. Scope health monitor should check capability/backend type before enabling scope
  2. Serial read errors should have exponential backoff (100ms → 200ms → 400ms → max 5s)

docs.title: Reproduction
docs.text:
uv run python3 -c '
# Launch web server for IC-7300 serial
from icom_lan.backends.config import SerialBackendConfig
# ... connect, start web on port 8081
# → check /tmp/ic7300-web.log
'

docs.title: Impact
docs.text:
• High CPU usage (34%)
  • Log files grow to GB in minutes
  • Serial port may become unresponsive

docs.title: Workaround
docs.text:
Manually disable scope before starting web server: 

radio._scope_enabled = False

docs.title: ✅ #262: refactor: move polling config to TOML rig profiles (data-driven)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Hardcoded Python dicts (_PROFILES, _SINGLE_CAPS, _DUAL_CAPS, _CMD29_7610) duplicate what's already in TOML rig profiles. This caused real bugs: - IC-7300 missing nb, nr, attenuator, preamp capabilities (commit 60c01eb) - Polling intervals hardcoded with _is_serial / has_lan checks instead of profile data - State queries gated on supports_cmd29() — but IC-7300 needs plain CI-V fallback

Single source of truth = TOML. Everything else derives from it.

docs.title: 1. Capabilities (currently hardcoded _SINGLE_CAPS / _DUAL_CAPS)
docs.text:
[capabilities]
features = ["audio", "scope", "meters", "tx", "cw", "attenuator", "preamp", "nb", "nr", ...]

docs.title: 2. Polling config (currently hardcoded _GAP_SERIAL, _FAST_INTERVAL_SERIAL)
docs.text:
[polling]
gap_ms = 50
meter_interval_ms = 200
transceive = true

docs.title: 3. cmd29 routes (currently hardcoded _CMD29_7610)
docs.text: Already partially in TOML as [cmd29] but not wired to runtime.

docs.title: 4. Profile resolution
docs.text: get_radio_profile() / resolve_radio_profile() must load from TOML via rig_loader.py, not from _PROFILES dict.

docs.title: Implementation Plan
docs.text:
1. Extend rig_loader.py to parse [polling] section → RigConfig
  2. profiles.py: get_radio_profile() loads TOML via rig_loader, caches result
  3. Remove _PROFILES, _SINGLE_CAPS, _DUAL_CAPS, _CMD29_7610
  4. RadioPoller.__init__ reads intervals from profile
  5. State query builder reads capabilities and cmd29 routes from profile
  6. Update ic7300.toml and ic7610.toml with [polling] section

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• Zero hardcoded radio-specific Python constants
  • Adding a new radio = adding one TOML file (no Python changes)
  • All existing tests pass (zero regressions)

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Commit 60c01eb (hotfix: _SINGLE_CAPS missing nb/nr/att/preamp)
  • Commit 5ea7915 (hotfix: serial polling intervals)
  • Epic #251 (rig profiles)

docs.title: ✅ #263: feat: USB Audio CODEC bridge for serial-connected radios (IC-7300)
docs.text: Labels: enhancement

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: IC-7300 connected via USB exposes a standard USB Audio Class device ("USB Audio CODEC") for RX/TX audio. Current Web UI audio pipeline only works with LAN UDP audio (IC-7610).

docs.title: USB Audio CODEC
docs.text:
macOS sees it as a standard audio input/output: - Input (mic): RX audio from radio - Output (speaker): TX audio to radio

This is NOT a serial port — it's a proper USB Audio Class 1.0 device.

docs.title: Implementation Plan
docs.text:
1. Use sounddevice or pyaudio to capture from "USB Audio CODEC" input
  2. Encode to Opus (or raw PCM 16-bit mono)
  3. Stream to frontend via existing WebSocket audio path
  4. Frontend Web Audio API decodes and plays

TX path (reverse): 1. Frontend captures mic → Opus encode → WS 2. Backend decodes → plays to "USB Audio CODEC" output

docs.title: Configuration (TOML)
docs.text:
[audio]
mode = "usb"  # "lan" | "usb" | "none"
device_name = "USB Audio CODEC"
sample_rate = 48000
channels = 1

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• IC-7610 uses LAN UDP audio (existing implementation)
  • sounddevice is already an optional dependency (pip install icom-lan[bridge])

docs.title: ⬜ #265: epic: Web UI redesign — capability-driven radio panel
docs.text: Labels: enhancement, ui, area:web-ui

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Complete Web UI redesign based on the Pencil mockup (mockups/web-ui/radio-panel.pen) — a professional, dark-themed radio control interface inspired by Icom styling.

Key principle: capability-driven rendering. Every UI element maps to a TOML capability. No ATU → no ATU panel. No dual_rx → single VFO. The mockup represents the maximal UI (all capabilities ON); the frontend conditionally renders based on the connected radio's rig profile.

docs.title: Design Reference
docs.text:
• Mockup: mockups/web-ui/radio-panel.pen (Pencil format, 15k lines)
  • Screenshot: mockups/web-ui/radio-panel-screenshot.png
  • Design language: Dark operational UI, Roboto Mono, cyan/orange/red accent system
  • Key colors: bg #0B0E12, accent #00D4FF, TX #FF6A00, danger #FF2020, ok #00CC66

• [ ] LinearSMeter.svelte — horizontal bar S1-S9/+20/+40/+60, animated, CSS/canvas
  • [ ] FrequencyDisplay.svelte — large mono freq with dot separators
  • [ ] SegmentedButton.svelte — reusable multi-option toggle (ATT, AGC, PREAMP, etc.)
  • [ ] StatusBadge.svelte — pill badge with active/inactive states

  • [ ] Slider.svelte — horizontal slider with numeric readout

  • [ ] VfoOps.svelte — A↔B, A→B, Split, TX routing
  • [ ] RfFrontEnd.svelte — RF Gain, ATT, Preamp (gated: rf_gain, attenuator, preamp)
  • [ ] AgcPanel.svelte — AGC mode + decay slider
  • [ ] FilterPanel.svelte — Width, Shift, PBT (gated: pbt presence in commands)
  • [ ] RitXitPanel.svelte — RIT/XIT on/off + freq offset (gated: RIT commands)
  • [ ] DspPanel.svelte — NR, NB, Notch, CW Auto Tune (gated: nr, nb)
  • [ ] TxPanel.svelte — PTT state, Mic Gain, ATU, VOX, COMP (gated: tx)

  • [ ] RxAudioPanel.svelte — monitor mode, AF level, browser audio (gated: audio)
  • [ ] BandSelector.svelte — band grid from TOML freq_ranges (existing, restyle)

  • [ ] Dual VFO header (conditional on dual_rx)
  • [ ] Bottom dual-RX strip with mini-spectrums (conditional on dual_rx)
  • [ ] CSS variables / design tokens extracted from mockup
  • [ ] Dark theme as default (match mockup palette)
  • [ ] Responsive: desktop-first, tablet/mobile degrade gracefully

docs.text:

  • [ ] Keyboard shortcuts integration
  • [ ] Touch-friendly controls for tablet
  • [ ] Mobile layout adaptation

docs.text:
May need to add explicit features to [capabilities]: - rit, xit — currently implied by commands presence - tuner — ATU support - pbt — passband tuning - split — split operation - notch — manual/auto notch

Alternative: derive from [commands] section (if set_rit_status exists → RIT is available).

docs.text:

  • Pencil batch_get / snapshot_layout → extract exact design params (colors, sizes, fonts)
  • Existing frontend/src/ as reference for API/WS/store integration
  • Canvas components (Spectrum, Waterfall) — keep existing, restyle container only
  • One component per PR/commit for reviewability

docs.title: ⬜ #266: feat(ui): LinearSMeter atomic component

Create a reusable LinearSMeter.svelte atomic component — a horizontal bar meter matching the Pencil mockup design.

Parent: #265 (Web UI redesign epic)

  • [ ] Cyan gradient for S1-S9, orange-red for over-S9
